Our Global Recognition
Where Our Graduates Work.
Our graduates thrive in business and arts, often continuing their studies or launching ventures. We highlight companies hiring them and our robust alumni network.
Ratih Megasari Singkarru, M.Sc.,
Ratih Megasari Singkarru, M.Sc., is an Indonesian politician and House of Representatives member (2019-2024). A 2008 Communication Science graduate from President University, she is also President Director of PT Ratih Graha Persada and PT Ratih Graha Cipta, and Commissioner of PT Dirga Mega Cipta.
Felicia Putri Tjiasaka
Felicia Putri Tjiasaka, a management alumna, is a content creator and Co-Founder of Ternak Uang, known for her investment content on social media. She was a runner-up at the 2015 DSB Asian Conference-Young Economist Stand Up.
Irene Umar
Irene Umar is an outstanding alumna of the Management Program at President University. With extensive experience in finance, investment, and social sectors through her roles at Standard Chartered Bank and W3GG, she currently serves as Vice Minister/Deputy Head of Creative Economy of the Republic of Indonesia.
Graha Yudha Andarano Putra P
After graduating, Graha Yudha worked in finance, including at Citi Bank. By age 33, he joined the boards of state-owned enterprises and became a member of Indonesia’s Presidential Advisory Council, a role he still holds today. He holds a B.Sc. from President University, an MBA from Strathclyde, and is a PhD candidate at the University of Glasgow’s Adam Smith Business School.
Darren Suciono
After graduating, Darren immediately entered the field of Search Engine Optimization. Working closely with his team, he played a pivotal role in leading PT Sumber Sinergi Makmur through its Initial Public Offering (IPO) in October 2023, where he serves as the Chairman of the board. He holds a B.Sc. in Industrial Engineering from President University.

The History
The conceptual plan of President University was first formulated in September 1997 by S. D. Darmono, the Chairman of Jababeka Group and Prof. Donald W. Watts, who was the President of Bond University, Queensland and Vice Chancellor of Curtin University, Western Australia. The goal of their proposed University was to create an institution of learning which would prepare future leaders of industry and society by giving them the skills and experience necessary to excel upon graduation.
The institution officially operated in 2001, offering a Bachelor of Engineering degree. At that time, the institution was called the Cikarang School of Engineering (Sekolah Tinggi Teknik Cikarang). On April 16, 2004, the Ministry of Education granted President University official status as a full-fledged university.
Although as a relatively young university, President University (PresUniv) is growing at a tremendous rate every year. There are now approximately 10,000 students attending President University from Indonesia as well as many other countries.
President University has expanded its academic disciplines and now offers 26 majors in 7 different faculties, ranging from Art, Design & Architecture, Business, Artificial Intelligence and Smart Manufacturing, Engineering, Law, Medicine, and Social Science & Education.
Vision And Mission
Vision
"To be an eminent university that produces leaders with entrepreneurial spirit, multicultural and global mindset."
Mission
1
To establish an education system with a high standard of excellence
2
Strengthen and encourage self-development to become creative, innovative, inclusive and broad-minded leaders
3
Conduct high quality, effective and sustainable research and community service
4
Develop professional culture in managing the university resources
5
Establish mutually beneficial cooperation with various national, regional and international/global institutions in implementing the Tridharma of Higher Education
Our Founders
Setyono Djuandi Darmono
S.D. Darmono as Founder and Chairman Jababeka Group, as well as founder and Chairman Board of Trustee of President University Foundation. He is the Chairman of PATA (Pacific Asia Tourism Association), Chairman The Jakarta Old Town Revitalization Corporation, Chairman Tidar Heritage Foundation, Chairman IJIN (Indonesia Japan Investors Network), Chairman IABC (Indonesia Australia Business Council) in 2008 – 2013, Chairman PT. TWC Borobudur Prambanan UNESCO World Culture Heritage in 2008 – 2013. He is the recipient of Ernst & Young Awards (2004), Indonesia CEO’s Choice of The Year of The Indonesia Business Leader Awards – CNBC (2005)
Prof. Dr. Juwono Sudarsono
Prof. Dr. Juwono Sudarsono served as Vice Governor of Lemhannas 1995-1998; Minister of State for the Environment under President Soeharto, 1998; Minister for Education and Culture under President B.J. Habibie, 1998-1999; Minister for Defense under President Abdurrahman Wahid , 1999-2000; Indonesian Ambassador for the United Kingdom under President Megawati Soekarnoputri, 2003-2004; and Minister for Defense under President Susilo Bambang Yudhoyono, 2004-2009. He now serves as one of the honorary advisors to President University
Prof. Dr. Charles Himawan, S.H., LL.M.
Charles Himawan is Indonesia’s first doctor of Juridical Science (Legal Science) graduated from Harvard University Law School, United States (US) in 1979. He took his Legal Law Master (LLM) degree at Harvard University Law School, US in 1976 and the undergraduate program of the Faculty of Law, University of Indonesia, 1961. Throughout his career he has been the dean of the University of Indonesia, faculty of law, a member of the national human rights commission and one of the founders of the University of Indonesia Center for Legal and Economic Studies.
Drs. Utomo Josodirdjo
Drs. Utomo Josodirdjo is one of the founders of President University who is also known as the “Father of Indonesian Accounting”. He was born in 1930 and passed away in 2022. Utomo had a strong educational background, with degrees from Erasmus University, University of Indonesia, and Harvard University. He was active in the development of modern accounting systems in Indonesia and was once a member of the Honorary Board of the Indonesian Accountants Association. In addition, he also has a hobby of flying and has been a pilot, taught at Airlangga University, and traveled on business to several cities in Indonesia and Malaysia.
Ir. Laksamana Sukardi
Ir. Laksamana Sukardi (born October 1, 1956) is an Indonesian economist and politician. He served as Minister of State for State-Owned Enterprises from 1999 to 2000 and from 2001 to 2004. Before becoming a politician, he was one of the economists and bankers owned by Bank Lippo and LippoGroup. He joined the Indonesian Democratic Party (PDI) then the Indonesian Democratic Party of Struggle (PDI-P) and the Democratic Renewal Party (PDP).
Surjanto Sosrodjojo, Dipl., Kfm.

Handa S. Abidin, S.H., LL.M., Ph.D.
Rector of President University
Before serving as the Rector of President University, Dr. Abidin had been a vice rector at   University since 2016. He has held various positions as vice rector, with his most recent role being Vice Rector for Academics. As an academic, he has research interest in international law and climate change law.
He teaches courses inter alia: “International Climate Change Law”. His peer-reviewed book (monograph) entitled “The Protection of Indigenous Peoples and Reduction of Forest Carbon Emissions: The REDD-Plus Regime and International Law” is owned by the world’s top law schools: Harvard Law School (Harvard Law School Library), Yale Law School (Lillian Goldman Law Library), and Stanford Law School (Robert Crown Law Library). Dr. Abidin has been trusted by the Asian Legal Business (Thomson Reuters) as the judge of the prestigious ALB SE Asia Law Awards 2018-2023 and the ALB Indonesia Law Awards 2015 & 2017-2023.
Dr. Abidin has three law degrees from three different countries: Indonesia, the USA, and the UK. He received his Sarjana Hukum (S.H.) degree from Universitas Indonesia, his Master of Laws (LL.M.) degree from the George Washington University Law School, and his Ph.D. degree from the University of Edinburgh Law School.
Vice-Rector
Dr. Adhi Setyo Santoso, ST., MBA.
Vice-Rector for Academic Affairs, Research, and Innovation
Before being appointed Vice-Rector for Academic Affairs, Research, and Innovation at President University, Dr. Adhi Setyo Santoso, S.T., M.BA., has been a member of the Presuniv academic staff since 2016 and has served in several leadership roles within the university, including Director of Setsail BizAccel (the university’s business incubator) and Director of the President Research Center.
An active scholar in digital business and innovation, Dr. Adhi’s research focuses on platform strategy, open innovation, and entrepreneurship in multi-sided digital platforms; his recent publications examine effectuation, open innovation processes, and strategic alliances for digital startups. He teaches across the Business Administration undergraduate program and the Master of Technology Management programs, bringing his research into courses on innovation management, digital business, and entrepreneurship.
Dr. Adhi holds a Doctoral degree from Universitas Indonesia, an M.B.A. from KAIST, and a Sarjana Teknik (S.T.) from Institut Teknologi Bandung. His appointment as Vice-Rector was announced by President University in 2024, where colleagues and the campus community have recognized his vision for strengthening the university’s research, innovation, and industry-linked teaching.
Prof. Dr. Retnowati, STh., MSi.
Vice-Rector for Resources
Before being appointed Vice-Rector for Resources at President University, Prof. Dr. Retnowati, S.Th., M.Si. served as a senior member of the President University academic staff and has played an active role in the university’s teaching and academic community. She was formally conferred the title of full professor by President University in mid-2024.
An active scholar in the fields of entrepreneurship and management, Prof. Retnowati teaches courses such as entrepreneurship, corporate entrepreneurship, human capital, and business ethics. Her teaching connects theory with practice, supporting student innovation and ethical leadership across the Business Administration program.
Prof. Retnowati holds a doctorate from Universitas Indonesia, a M.Si. from Universitas Gadjah Mada, and a S.Th. from Universitas Kristen Duta Wacana. Her promotion to professorship and subsequent appointment as Vice-Rector for Resources reflect her academic achievements and leadership within the PresUniv community.
Prof. Dr. Ir. Chairy, S.E., M.M.
Vice Rector for Partnership
Before serving as Rector of President University for the 2022–2024 period, Prof. Dr. Ir. Chairy, S.E., M.M. was a long-standing member of the President University faculty and a senior leader within the campus community, recognized for his administrative leadership and commitment to developing industry-connected teaching and research.
An active scholar in marketing and management, Prof. Chairy’s research focuses on consumer behavior, branding, retailing, and green marketing; his work also examines topics such as green consumption, hospitality and tourism marketing, and customer brand engagement. He regularly teaches marketing and management courses within the Faculty of Business, and his research has appeared in national and international journals and conference outlets.
Prof. Chairy holds a doctoral degree in Management from Universitas Indonesia and carries professional training that combines engineering and business credentials (the honorific “Ir.” indicating an engineering background, alongside his S.E. and M.M. degrees). His extensive publication record and active involvement in research networks reflect his ongoing contribution to marketing scholarship and to strengthening President University’s academic profile.
International Chancellor
Prof. Ki-chan Kim
International Chancellor
Before being appointed International Chancellor of President University, Prof. Ki-Chan Kim served as a senior faculty member in management at the Catholic University of Korea and held several leadership roles there, including dean of the Business School and vice president. His formal introduction as President University’s International Chancellor took place in February 2023.
Prof. Kim is an active scholar and global voice on entrepreneurship and humane, people-centered business practices. His work covers topics such as humane entrepreneurship, platform strategy, modularization and inter-firm relationships, and he has advised government bodies and major companies in Korea. He also chairs and holds leadership roles in international organizations focused on small business and entrepreneurship, amplifying his influence beyond academia.
President University Foundation (YPUP)
Prof. Dr. Ir. Budi Susilo Soepandji
Chairperson of President University Foundation
Prof. Dr. Ir. Budi Susilo Soepandji serves as the Executive Chairman of President University Foundation. He is an academic and bureaucrat who has served as Governor of Lemhanas RI from 2011 to 2016. In addition, he has also been a Professor of Civil Engineering in Geotechnics at the University of Indonesia since 1998. Budi Susilo is also known as a lover of art and culture, especially wayang kulit and music. He has initiated several wayang kulit performances and formed the band Professor Band with several professors at the University of Indonesia. In his educational and governmental career, he has served as Head of the Soil Mechanics Laboratory, Head of the Civil Engineering Department, Director of the Technology Institute of the University of Indonesia, Assistant Dean, and Dean of the Faculty of Engineering of the University of Indonesia from 2000 to 2004.
Prof. Dr. Ir. Chairy, S.E., M.M.
Secretary of President University Foundation
Prof. Dr. Ir. Chairy, S.E., M.M. serves as the Secretary of the President University Foundation. He has a multidisciplinary academic background, earning an Ir. in Civil Engineering from Institut Sains dan Teknologi Nasional, as well as an S.E., M.M., and Doctorate in Marketing from the University of Indonesia. He was appointed as Professor through the Decree of the Minister of Education and Culture of the Republic of Indonesia. Chairy also participated in the Global Faculty Visitor Program at the Kauffman Foundation, Kansas City, USA. In his academic leadership career, he previously served as the Rector of President University and has contributed significantly to academic development and strategic management within the university and the foundation.
Justin Endramukti
Treasurer of President University Foundation
Justin Endramukti serves as the Treasurer of President University Foundation

It combines accommodation with programs that strengthen academic progress, leadership skills, and social development. PUSH consists of several residential sites, including SBH (Student Boarding House) and NBH (New Beverly Hills).

Internship & Career Center
What's Next After Graduation?
Our internship program serves as a facility for students to gain practical experience. By joining taking an internship program, students will learn and expose themselves to the variety of roles, functions, and abundance of opportunities in industries. This exposure generates a list of “career prospects” for the students, which will lead them to the answer of “what’s next after graduation”.
The quality of our graduates are measured by the abilities to create impact for the community and are reflected from their portfolios. This portfolio is made possible because as interns, our students will be part of the innovation and problem solving process where they can showcase their discipline, personal traits, knowledge and skills to the hiring manager and grow their professional network. This portfolio will be proof of their proficiency and adds value to them as professionals.
Why Internship?
In the end, our internship goal is “employability” which may be translated as the ability of our graduates to land their dream career as how they want it, when they need it. However, employability goes beyond getting hired, it is about constantly innovating and match the competencies with what industry or hiring organizations look for in a candidate. Combined with the academic mastery, the internship portfolio often helps students secure their job before graduation and stay relevant in a dynamic job market.
Internships are not just about fulfilling requirements, it is also about opening doors to the world of possibilities. Our internship journey shapes many success stories of graduates being offered with job opportunities right after they completed their internship.
Ready, Set, Get Hired
Resume Mastery
President University’s web-based Resume Builder guides you step by step through every section of your resume. Capture your key achievements, projects, work experience and certifications in one place. Each year the Career Center reviews your document to keep it aligned with current industry needs. The review involves personal coaching session to polish the final details, condense information, and shape a clear professional layout. The result is a concise one-page resume that you can use to apply for internships and jobs from your first semester, and one that stays competitive right up to graduation.
Internship & Job Opportunity Portal
One account gives you access to a curated list of internships and job openings from hundreds of partner companies. The portal (
https://linktr.ee/icc_pu
) is designed to boost employability by delivering a steady flow of high-quality opportunities for both students and alumni.
Career Fair
Our annual job fair brings many companies to campus and lets you apply and interview on the same day. We also host regular Campus Hiring events, each focused on a single employer for a more targeted recruitment experience. These programs combined, create a direct path from a strong resume to a signed employment contract.

International Office
The International Office at President University is a dedicated department that handles all matters related to international students and mobility programs. Our primary functions include:
Visa and Immigration Support
Guiding visa applications, renewals, and compliance with immigration regulations, ensuring students maintain their legal status while studying.
Student Mobility Program
Promoting international collaboration and opportunities for students and faculty. Assisting international students with the application and admission processes related to the student mobility program, ensuring that all necessary documents and requirements are met.
Detailed information is provided in our guide book for the Outbound Program and the Inbound Program. We also provide an Outbound Program Calendar for our students to find dates and deadlines for mobility programs offered by our partner universities.
Universities our students have visited for Outbound Program:
Countries of students that has joined our Inbound Program at President University:
International Student Services
Offering assistance for academic, social, and personal issues, including counseling services, language assistance, and cultural exchange programs.
Detailed information is provided in our guide book for International Students.
Program and Events for International Students
The International Office is active in conducting program and events that aim to introduce and blend cultures from and for our international students. Among them are:
President University Buddy Program, where we collaborate with the Student Affairs Bureau to match international students with Indonesian students. The aim is to facilitate students in exchanging knowledge about each other's language and culture.
Cultural Festival, where the students both international and local can mingle and showcase their culture in the biggest cultural show on campus.
President University International Dinners, where we invite our international students to introduce their culture through cuisines that they prepare and serve for the invitees.
Beyond the above programs, we also regularly host smaller events that include cultural movie night and cultural trips to various cultural and historical sites around Indonesia aimed to give international students exposure to Indonesian culture and history.
Scholarships for Inbound and Outbound Mobility Program
President University has provided scholarships for international students since its establishment in 2001. In the last decade, however, the International Office has provided numerous inbound international students with scholarship opportunities and programs.
Scholarships such as DAAD's EMPM program, the Indonesian Aid Scholarship (TIAS), and the South East Asia Leadership program are among the ones we provide through collaborations with the relevant institutions.
Aside of that,  there are also many scholarship opportunities available for Indonesian students to join our outbound programs like CMK Hyundai, and numerous other partners for exchange program.

Student Affairs
Student Affairs Bureau is the core that energizes campus life, dedicated to nurturing every student into a well-rounded leader. Student Affairs does not simply manage services, but also actively cultivates an ecosystem full of opportunities and support.
Our mission goes beyond managing activities. It's about embedding the core values of President University, especially the
I.M.P.A.C.T.
philosophy, into every aspect of campus life. We are the architects of a dynamic, multicultural, and high-impact student experience. Our key areas of focus include:
Student Organizations
Fostering and guiding student organizations (PUSC, PUSB, PUFA, PUMA), clubs and communities (CnC). Overseeing major events and leadership training. Develops leadership, teamwork, and organizational skills as we provides avenues for expression and contribution to campus life.
Student Events
Through President University Student Council (PUSC) and President University Student Board (PUSB), we host vibrant and high-impact events designed to challenge student to develop essential skills, and celebrate the university’s unique Culture.
These student-driven initiatives, which form a vital part of the Portfolio-Building Curriculum, include the intensive President Youth Leadership Camp (
PYLC)
, the artistic showcase of President University Vocal Victory
(PUVV)
, the industry insights platform
(INTERACT)
, the competitive spirit of President University Sport Tournament
(PUST)
, the global celebration of Cultural Festival
(
CULFEST), and the culminating recognition at the Student Awarding Night
(SAN)
.
These events are crucial for fostering collaboration among students and implementing the Medium of Communication in English in a dynamic, fun setting.
Competition and Scholarship
We encourage student involvement in competitions or the hosting of national and international level events, providing unmatched real-world experience and networking opportunities.We actively facilitate student applications for prestigious scholarships provided by our industry and government partners, including:
a. Adaro Foundation Scholarship:
A program supporting students in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) fields, focusing on academic excellence and leadership potential.
b. China Harbour Engineering Company (CHEC)
Scholarship:
Financial aid specifically aimed at students demonstrating high academic merit, often prioritizing those in relevant engineering or technical disciplines.
c. Beasiswa Pemerintah Daerah (Banpin Pemda) Bekasi
Scholarships provided by the Bekasi Local Government to support outstanding students who are residents of Bekasi, promoting local talent and development.
d. Diktisaintek Berdampak
Diktisaintek Berdampak is a continuous program for univeristy students across Indonesia. The program include:
PKM
(Program Kreativitas Mahasiswa),
P2MW
(student entrepreneurship),
PPK Ormawa
(Student organization),
FLC
(Future Leaders Camp),
KKN
Kebangsaan
,
Magang Berdampak
,
PILMAPRES
, and
Diktisaintek Berdampak Internasional
.
Support & Welfare
Providing essential services such as development programs, counseling referrals, and maintaining student records.
Ethical Conduct & Policy
Developing and implementing non-academic policies, including student conduct procedures, to ensure a respectful and orderly campus environment.
Student Life
Student life at President University is vibrant and community-focused. PUSH, the university dormitory, offers a supportive living environment for students. Meanwhile, PUSU (student union) and CnC (Clubs and Communities) provide opportunities for leadership, creativity, and meaningful engagement on campus.

Endowment Fund
Securing Education’s Future by Investing in Endowment Today.
About
About
Endowment Fund
President University Endowment Fund is a program to raise funds through contributions from student, alumni, the academic community, companies, philanthropists and other sources. Then the funds are invested and the results are used to support the sustainability of President University in the future.
Grand
Total
IDR 576,101,010
IDR 110,250,000
Additional Contribution in 2026
IDR 465,851,010
As of December 31, 2025
How to Donate:
Making an endowment donation for President University is easy. Use the QR Code attached below or you can also transfer it manually through our Bank Account and please save your transaction proof and send it to our email at alumni@president.ac.id (include your name and phone number).
Scan for Donate
Bank Name
Mandiri
Account Number
1560020011021
Account Name
Yayasan Pend Universitas Presiden
Bank Name
Bank Central Asia
Account Number
8730256222
Account Name
Yay Pendidikan Univ Presiden
Who Can Contribute:
Alumni
Since its founding, President University has produced more than 11.000 alumni spread across the world. Alumni are a very valuable asset for President University. The active participation of alumni in the progress of their alma mater is highly expected.
Company
Company is a strategic partner for President University. Industry will later absorb the human resources produced by President University. Therefore, President University really hopes that Industry will participate in investing in human resource development in the future through President University donations.
Organization
Organizations or communities can also contribute to the progress of President University. Various organizations and communities have shown their commitment to raising funds for President University.
Academic Community
Both lecturers and staff are also expected to be able to contribute, either donating funds or initiating fundraising movements in order to increase the President University Endowment Fund.
Public
President University really hopes for joint contributions from the wider community who care about higher education. Any individual can contribute by donating some of their funds for the development of science and technology.
The aims of
Endowment Fund
This endowment fund is designed to provide support for the continuity of President University's operations, not only for the current generation but also for the future. With the determination to maintain the continuity of existence across generations, the management of these funds will be carried out carefully, ensuring that the benefits can be enjoyed by future generations. Some of the objectives of this endowment fund are as attached below:
Providing Scholarships
Part of the funds from the Endowment Fund will be used to provide scholarships to students with achievements and potential, ensuring access to quality education without financial barriers.
Academic and Research Development
Funds will be allocated to support research activities, seminars and workshops to improve academic excellence and produce innovations that are beneficial to society.
Facilities and Infrastructure Development
A portion of the funds will be invested in developing campus facilities and infrastructure, creating a modern and adequate learning environment.

Research
1. Patents
President University actively supports innovation and intellectual property development through its research and academic community. Faculty members and students are encouraged to transform their ideas into practical solutions, many of which have been successfully registered as national and international patents. These patents reflect the university’s commitment to fostering creativity, problem-solving, and contributions to industry and society through original inventions and technologies.
Discover
2. Grants
To enhance research excellence, President University regularly secures competitive research grants from government bodies, international institutions, and industry partners. These grants enable faculty and students to conduct impactful research across various disciplines, from science and technology to social sciences and education. The university also provides internal funding schemes to support early-stage research, prototype development, and community-based projects.
Discover
3. Publications
Scholarly book publication is a key pillar of President University’s research output. Faculty members contribute to the academic discourse by publishing books that offer fresh perspectives, critical analyses, and in-depth explorations across diverse fields. These works are often used as references in classrooms, libraries, and academic forums, strengthening the university’s role as a knowledge-producing institution.
Discover
3. Books
President University promotes a strong culture of academic writing and journal publication among its faculty and students. Research articles are regularly published in reputable national and international journals, covering a wide array of topics and disciplines. This commitment to scholarly publication not only enhances the university’s research visibility but also contributes to global academic and scientific advancement.
Discover

Faculty Of Business
At the Faculty of Business at President University, we cultivate visionary leaders who convert ideas into significant solutions, fostering innovation and influencing the future of business in a dynamic global environment.
About Us
The Faculty of Business at President University is committed to developing future leaders and innovators who will influence the business landscape.  We are dedicated to delivering a vibrant and complete educational experience that integrates theoretical knowledge with practical application.  Our faculty comprises highly accomplished professionals and scholars dedicated to cultivating critical thinking, creativity, and ethical leadership.
Our programs are structured to furnish students with the competencies and mentality essential for success in the swiftly evolving global business landscape.  Through innovative research, practical case studies, and experiential projects, our students acquire a profound comprehension of business ideas and methods, equipping them for success across diverse industries.
At President University’s Faculty of Business, we strive to establish a robust foundation for our students, enabling them to emerge as influential business leaders who effectuate positive transformation globally.
Prof. Dr. Anton Wachidin Widjaja, S.E.,M.M.
Dean of The Faculty of Business
Google Scholar
SINTA
ORCID
“The Faculty of Business provides high-quality education that ensures our graduates become successful professionals or entrepreneurs in their chosen fields of interest. Our faculty has a higher number of foreign students that collaborate with local students in academic study and curricular activities, ensuring multi-cultural learning.
You will achieve your goal to become a top-class graduate, professional, or entrepreneur in a year. We will ensure you have the skills and competence to excel in your chosen career or profession.”
- Prof. Dr. Anton Wachidin Widjaja, S.E.,M.M.
See Our Graduate Major
Discover our diverse undergraduate majors that combine business theory with practical skills, preparing you for success in areas like management, marketing, finance, and entrepreneurship.
Accounting
Business Administration
Management
Actuarial Science
Agribusiness
Where Our Graduates Work.
Our graduates thrive in business and arts, often continuing their studies or launching ventures. We highlight companies hiring them and our robust alumni network.
Apply Now!
TIAS Scholarship
Ratih Megasari Singkarru, M.Sc.,
Ratih Megasari Singkarru, M.Sc., is an Indonesian politician and House of Representatives member (2019-2024). A 2008 Communication Science graduate from President University, she is also President Director of PT Ratih Graha Persada and PT Ratih Graha Cipta, and Commissioner of PT Dirga Mega Cipta.
Felicia Putri Tjiasaka
Felicia Putri Tjiasaka, a management alumna, is a content creator and Co-Founder of Ternak Uang, known for her investment content on social media. She was a runner-up at the 2015 DSB Asian Conference-Young Economist Stand Up.
Irene Umar
Irene Umar is an outstanding alumna of the Management Program at President University. With extensive experience in finance, investment, and social sectors through her roles at Standard Chartered Bank and W3GG, she currently serves as Vice Minister/Deputy Head of Creative Economy of the Republic of Indonesia.
Graha Yudha Andarano Putra P
After graduating, Graha Yudha worked in finance, including at Citi Bank. By age 33, he joined the boards of state-owned enterprises and became a member of Indonesia’s Presidential Advisory Council, a role he still holds today. He holds a B.Sc. from President University, an MBA from Strathclyde, and is a PhD candidate at the University of Glasgow’s Adam Smith Business School.
Darren Suciono
After graduating, Darren immediately entered the field of Search Engine Optimization. Working closely with his team, he played a pivotal role in leading PT Sumber Sinergi Makmur through its Initial Public Offering (IPO) in October 2023, where he serves as the Chairman of the board. He holds a B.Sc. in Industrial Engineering from President University.
Take the next step toward your personal and professional goals with President University.

Faculty Of Computer Science
At the Faculty of Computer Science at President University, we cultivate innovative tech professionals who transform ideas into cutting-edge digital solutions, driving progress and shaping the future of technology in a rapidly evolving digital world.
About Us
Established in 2002, the Faculty of Computer Science at President University is dedicated to developing future-ready technology professionals who can lead and innovate in the digital era. Our programs, including Informatics and Information Systems, are designed to provide a balanced education that integrates theoretical foundations with practical applications, ensuring our graduates are well-equipped to meet industry demands.
Our curriculum is meticulously designed to meet the evolving needs of the industry, adopting both theoretical and hands-on approaches to learning. This ensures students acquire the knowledge and skills needed to excel in the workplace. To facilitate an effective learning environment, classes are limited in size, and professionals from external institutions are often invited as guest lecturers to expose students to current industry practices.
The Faculty of Computer Science is committed to fostering a learning environment that emphasizes innovation, critical thinking, and ethical responsibility. Through collaborations with industry partners and a focus on real-world problem-solving, our students engage in projects that prepare them to address complex challenges in various computing fields.
At President University's Faculty of Computer Science, we strive to build a strong foundation for our students, enabling them to become influential tech leaders who drive positive transformation in the global digital landscape.
Prof. Dr. Ir. Wiranto Herry Utomo, M.Kom.
Dean of The Faculty of Computer Science
Google Scholar
SINTA
ORCID
"Welcome to the Faculty of Computer Science. We are delighted that you are visiting us and happy to provide you with the information you need. Prospective students from Indonesia and abroad are invited to consider studying here. Faculty of Computer Science provides many attractive opportunities for students. Our school was established in 2002. Since then our students, faculty, staff, and alumni continue to contribute in significant ways to our reputation as a very strong school for the study of computer science. Once you decide to invest in us, we will take that very seriously. Hold us accountable for meeting your needs as you pursue your dream, and we guide you to becoming a member of the computer-related profession. Finally, if you join us, it will be a time of exciting challenges as well as academic and personal growth for you. We are here to help you overcome the challenges and obtain your degree.”.
- Prof. Dr. Ir. Wiranto Herry Utomo, M.Kom.

Ratih Megasari Singkarru, M.Sc.,
Ratih Megasari Singkarru, M.Sc., is an Indonesian politician and House of Representatives member (2019-2024). A 2008 Communication Science graduate from President University, she is also President Director of PT Ratih Graha Persada and PT Ratih Graha Cipta, and Commissioner of PT Dirga Mega Cipta.
Felicia Putri Tjiasaka
Felicia Putri Tjiasaka, a management alumna, is a content creator and Co-Founder of Ternak Uang, known for her investment content on social media. She was a runner-up at the 2015 DSB Asian Conference-Young Economist Stand Up.
Irene Umar
Irene Umar is an outstanding alumna of the Management Program at President University. With extensive experience in finance, investment, and social sectors through her roles at Standard Chartered Bank and W3GG, she currently serves as Vice Minister/Deputy Head of Creative Economy of the Republic of Indonesia.
Graha Yudha Andarano Putra P
After graduating, Graha Yudha worked in finance, including at Citi Bank. By age 33, he joined the boards of state-owned enterprises and became a member of Indonesia’s Presidential Advisory Council, a role he still holds today. He holds a B.Sc. from President University, an MBA from Strathclyde, and is a PhD candidate at the University of Glasgow’s Adam Smith Business School.
Darren Suciono
After graduating, Darren immediately entered the field of Search Engine Optimization. Working closely with his team, he played a pivotal role in leading PT Sumber Sinergi Makmur through its Initial Public Offering (IPO) in October 2023, where he serves as the Chairman of the board. He holds a B.Sc. in Industrial Engineering from President University.
Take the next step toward your personal and professional goals with President University.

Faculty Of Engineering
At the Faculty of Engineering at President University, we cultivate innovative engineers who transform ideas into impactful solutions, driving technological advancement and shaping the future of industry.
About Us
The Faculty of Engineering at President University is committed to developing future engineers and innovators who will drive progress across various industries. We are dedicated to delivering a dynamic and comprehensive educational experience that blends strong theoretical foundations with hands-on application.
Our faculty consists of experienced professionals and distinguished scholars who are passionate about nurturing analytical thinking, innovation, and ethical engineering practices.
Our programs are designed to equip students with the technical competencies, problem-solving abilities, and mindset required to succeed in an increasingly complex and technology-driven world. Through applied research, industry-based projects, and experiential learning, our students gain a deep understanding of engineering principles and practices that prepare them for real-world challenges.
At President University’s Faculty of Engineering, we aim to build a solid foundation for our students, empowering them to become influential engineers who contribute to sustainable development and technological innovation on a global scale.
Ir. Yosef Barita Sar Manik, S.T., M.Sc, Ph.D
Dean of The Faculty of Engineering
Google Scholar
SINTA
ORCID
Dear all students and prospective students,
Engineering is identical to the concept of designing and improving for the advancement of human life. The Faculty of Engineering, President University, also lives this spirit. We are here to prepare you to become innovative and determined problem solvers. We are convinced that the synergy between our high-quality faculty members with extensive educational backgrounds and practical experience and your motivation to gain knowledge through hands-on laboratories and internship activities will equip you with the skills required by the users in your future career.
Let us share our aspirations as the Faculty strives to fulfill the vision to be at the forefront in producing graduates who excel in academics, professions, and entrepreneurship, and contribute to the progress of Indonesian society and the world.
- Ir. Yosef Barita Sar Manik, S.T., M.Sc, Ph.D

Faculty Of Social Science And Education
Explore our diverse undergraduate programs that combine critical thinking with cultural and historical insights, preparing you for success in fields such as communication, education, literature, history, and cultural studies.
About Us
The Faculty of Social Science and Education at President University is dedicated to exploring the complexities of human society, culture, and communication. Our programs, including Communication Science, International Relations, and Elementary Teacher Education, are designed to cultivate critical thinking, cultural awareness, and ethical leadership.
Located in the dynamic environment of the Jababeka Industrial Estate, the faculty offers students practical learning opportunities that connect academic theory with real-world application. Through strong partnerships with industry, government, and nonprofit organizations, students participate in internships, research projects, and community initiatives that enrich their academic experience.
Our faculty members are experienced educators and professionals who foster an engaging and intellectually stimulating environment. By emphasizing interdisciplinary learning and global perspectives, we prepare students to become thoughtful, informed, and impactful contributors to society.
At the Faculty of Social Science and Education, we are committed to nurturing well-rounded graduates who are academically strong, socially responsible, and ready to lead in a globalized world.

Faculty Of Law
At the Faculty of Law at President University, we cultivate principled legal professionals who transform knowledge into meaningful impact, promoting justice and shaping the future of law in a rapidly evolving global society.
About Us
The Law study program is designed to effectively introduce students to the workings of Indonesia’s complex legal system, and the fundamentals of legal studies within a duration of just over 3 years. The curriculum is adjusted in accordance with current trends in technology, business, and finance on a local and global scale.
To ensure deeper understanding, the courses offered adopts either a theoretical approach (through extensive lectures), hands-on approach (through projects and simulations), or both. They are delivered by qualified lecturers who have graduated from various top law schools in Indonesia and abroad, and have accumulated an abundance of field experience throughout the years.
In addition, numerous visits to governmental institutions, law firms, and other law-related institutions, and educational sessions with experts in the field shall ensure that students not only grasp the general idea of law, but also the current situation within the field at present.

Faculty Of Medicine
The Faculty of Medicine, President University, aims to produce dignified, high-quality graduates who are compassionate, ethical, and dedicated professionals.
About Us
President University's Faculty of Medicine, established in August 2023, has already achieved "Baik" accreditation, a testament to its rapid growth and commitment to excellence. Located in the heart of a thriving industrial hub, the faculty leverages its unique position to offer a specialized focus on Occupational Health.
The Center of Excellence for Occupational Health empowers students with practical experience and in-depth knowledge of workplace health challenges. The faculty's collaboration with Padjadjaran University fosters a comprehensive approach to public health through the Academic Health System. Together, they strive to shape the future of occupational health, nurturing future leaders, conducting groundbreaking research, and promoting worker well-being.
prof. dr. Budi Setiabudiawan, Sp.A(K), M.Kes.
Dean of The Faculty of Medicine
Google Scholar
SINTA
ORCID
The Faculty of Medicine, President University, aims to produce dignified, high-quality graduates who are compassionate, ethical, and dedicated professionals. They should prioritize patient safety and community needs while being adaptable to social environments and assisting communities in providing healthcare services. Being located in West Java, we also aim for each graduate to actively contribute to the success of the National Health Insurance (KIS/BPJS) program and primary healthcare services in the province.
- prof. dr. Budi Setiabudiawan, Sp.A(K), M.Kes.

Faculty Of Art, Design, And Architecture
At the Faculty of Art, Design, and Architecture at President University, we cultivate creative thinkers and visionary designers who transform ideas into impactful visual and spatial solutions, shaping the future of art, design, and the built environment in a rapidly evolving world.
About Us
The Faculty of Art, Design, and Architecture at President University is committed to developing future creators, designers, and architects who will shape the visual and built environments of tomorrow. We are dedicated to delivering a rich and holistic educational experience that integrates conceptual knowledge with hands-on practice.
Our faculty is composed of experienced artists, designers, and industry professionals who are passionate about fostering creativity, critical thinking, and ethical responsibility in the creative process.
Our programs are designed to equip students with the technical skills, artistic vision, and innovative mindset required to excel in the fast-evolving creative and design industries. Through studio work, real-world projects, and interdisciplinary collaboration, our students gain a deep understanding of design principles, visual communication, and architectural innovation.
At President University’s Faculty of Art, Design, and Architecture, we strive to provide a strong foundation for our students, empowering them to become influential creative professionals who inspire and lead positive change in local and global contexts.

Finally, if you join us, it will be a time of exciting challenges as well as academic and personal growth for you. We are here to help you overcome the challenges and obtain your degree.”.
- Prof. Dr. Ir. Wiranto Herry Utomo, M.Kom.

Ratih Megasari Singkarru, M.Sc.,
Ratih Megasari Singkarru, M.Sc., is an Indonesian politician and House of Representatives member (2019-2024). A 2008 Communication Science graduate from President University, she is also President Director of PT Ratih Graha Persada and PT Ratih Graha Cipta, and Commissioner of PT Dirga Mega Cipta.
Felicia Putri Tjiasaka
Felicia Putri Tjiasaka, a management alumna, is a content creator and Co-Founder of Ternak Uang, known for her investment content on social media. She was a runner-up at the 2015 DSB Asian Conference-Young Economist Stand Up.
Irene Umar
Irene Umar is an outstanding alumna of the Management Program at President University. With extensive experience in finance, investment, and social sectors through her roles at Standard Chartered Bank and W3GG, she currently serves as Vice Minister/Deputy Head of Creative Economy of the Republic of Indonesia.
Graha Yudha Andarano Putra P
After graduating, Graha Yudha worked in finance, including at Citi Bank. By age 33, he joined the boards of state-owned enterprises and became a member of Indonesia’s Presidential Advisory Council, a role he still holds today. He holds a B.Sc. from President University, an MBA from Strathclyde, and is a PhD candidate at the University of Glasgow’s Adam Smith Business School.
Darren Suciono
After graduating, Darren immediately entered the field of Search Engine Optimization. Working closely with his team, he played a pivotal role in leading PT Sumber Sinergi Makmur through its Initial Public Offering (IPO) in October 2023, where he serves as the Chairman of the board. He holds a B.Sc. in Industrial Engineering from President University.
Take the next step toward your personal and professional goals with President University.

Faculty Of Law
At the Faculty of Law at President University, we cultivate principled legal professionals who transform knowledge into meaningful impact, promoting justice and shaping the future of law in a rapidly evolving global society.
About Us
The Law study program is designed to effectively introduce students to the workings of Indonesia’s complex legal system, and the fundamentals of legal studies within a duration of just over 3 years. The curriculum is adjusted in accordance with current trends in technology, business, and finance on a local and global scale.
To ensure deeper understanding, the courses offered adopts either a theoretical approach (through extensive lectures), hands-on approach (through projects and simulations), or both. They are delivered by qualified lecturers who have graduated from various top law schools in Indonesia and abroad, and have accumulated an abundance of field experience throughout the years.
In addition, numerous visits to governmental institutions, law firms, and other law-related institutions, and educational sessions with experts in the field shall ensure that students not only grasp the general idea of law, but also the current situation within the field at present.

Faculty Of Business
At the Faculty of Business at President University, we cultivate visionary leaders who convert ideas into significant solutions, fostering innovation and influencing the future of business in a dynamic global environment.
About Us
The Doctor of Management program at the Faculty of Business is designed to nurture visionary leaders, researchers, and decision-makers who possess advanced expertise in management, innovation, and strategic leadership. This program offers a deep understanding of organizational dynamics and sustainable business practices, equipping candidates to address complex challenges in both local and global contexts.
The curriculum integrates contemporary trends in business, technology, and economics to ensure relevance and impact in today’s evolving landscape. Students experience a balanced approach that combines rigorous theoretical study with real-world application through seminars, research projects, and case analyses. This approach not only strengthens analytical and research capabilities but also fosters critical thinking and innovation in addressing managerial issues.
Courses are delivered by distinguished faculty members—graduates of leading universities in Indonesia and abroad—who bring rich academic backgrounds and extensive professional experience. The program also provides opportunities for research collaboration, academic exchange, and engagement with industry experts, enabling doctoral candidates to contribute impactful insights, lead organizational transformation, and advance the field of management with meaningful scholarship.
Assoc. Prof. Dr. Anton Wachidin Widjaja, SE, MM.
Dean of The Faculty of Business
Google Scholar
SINTA
ORCID
“The Faculty of Business provides high-quality education that ensures our graduates become successful professionals or entrepreneurs in their chosen fields of interest. Our faculty has a higher number of foreign students that collaborate with local students in academic study and curricular activities, ensuring multi-cultural learning.
You will achieve your goal to become a top-class graduate, professional, or entrepreneur in a year. We will ensure you have the skills and competence to excel in your chosen career or profession.”
- Assoc. Prof. Dr. Anton Wachidin Widjaja, SE, MM.

Accounting
About Us
The Accounting Study Program at President University equips students with the latest knowledge and practical skills required in the accounting profession. With strong academic foundations, practical exposure, and expert-led discussions, the program has consistently produced graduates who are highly sought after in the job market.
Many alumni secure employment shortly after graduation and continue to build successful careers with competitive income potential. Through hands-on lab classes, real-world case studies, and company visits, students gain valuable insights into current accounting practices. The program also integrates various accounting software tools to enhance students' digital competencies in the field.

Business Administration
About Us
The Business Administration program at President University is designed to foster an entrepreneurial mindset while offering students students practical exposure to a wide range of industries. This program also emphasizes global readiness by providing international learning opportunities and preparing students for postgraduate study abroad.
Students are encouraged to become business innovators, whether in the form of global business professionals, techno-preneurs or socio-preneurs. Through practical projects, internship streams and mobility options, students are able to experience firsthand how to build and lead in real business settings.

Management
About Us
President University's Management program produces versatile leaders ready for global business challenges, blending theory with practice in a multicultural English-taught environment. Key features include four concentrations—Banking & Finance (with Islamic Finance), Marketing (e-marketing focus), Human Capital Management (HR) and International Business—plus a final year stream for internships, scholarships, mobility, or startups. Students gain elite access via seminars, business plan training and partnerships with top firms.
Move Fast Advantage
: Complete 144 credits in an optimized special pathway, accelerating entry to workforce or grad school ahead of peers.
Jababeka Living Laboratory
: Immerse in Southeast Asia's largest industrial estate for projects and corporate exposure.
Professional Toolkits
: Includes digital marketing, financial analysis, HR strategies, negotiation and capstone projects like business ventures.

About Us
President University's Actuarial Science Program empowers students to master data, manage risk and build a secure future. With a curriculum aligned with professional certifications (PAI & SOA), the program emphasizes modern financial risk management through intensive training in data analytics, programming and actuarial modeling.
Delivered by experienced academics and industry practitioners, the program integrates theoretical knowledge with real-world projects, preparing graduates for high-demand careers in actuarial and financial fields.

He is currently working on the Splitting Method for PDE, focusing on stability, consistency, and convergence analysis, as well as its application in solving PDEs. His teaching expertise encompasses mathematical analysis and statistics, including Ordinary and Partial Differential Equations, Engineering Mathematics, Real Analysis, and Numerical Analysis.

Agribusiness
About Us
The Agribusiness Study Program at President University offers a
unique, technology-driven and sustainability-based approach
to modern agriculture through its focus on
Urban Integrated Farming (UIF).
This major integrates agribusiness systems from production to marketing with digital technologies such as IoT, data analytics and e-commerce, while emphasizing
entrepreneurship and global perspective
in an English-speaking academic environment.
There are
three key facts
about this major. First, it was officially established in 2021 and began admitting students in 2022. Second, it is strategically located in the Jababeka industrial area, enabling strong
industry visits, collaborative projects and internship programs
. Third, it is supported by a faculty affiliated with the Accreditation Council for Business Schools and Programs (ACBSP), ensuring international academic standards.
This major is
relevant, impactful and future-oriented
because it addresses food security and digital transformation challenges, provides
collaborative research, industry engagement and community empowerment programs,
and prepares graduates to become
digital agripreneurs and leaders in sustainable agribusiness
at national and global levels.
Duration
3 - 4 years
Degree
Sarjana Pertanian (S.P.)
Accreditation
Baik (BAN PT)
Curriculum Framework
Type
Subjects
Core Courses
Five most distinctive and highly relevant core courses that reflect the uniquenesses of the program are:
Agricultural Production Management -
Focused on efficient and sustainable production systems aligned with Urban Integrated Farming.
Agricultural Marketing -
Emphasizes value creation and digital market strategies for agribusiness products.
Supply Chain Management -
Integrates agrifood logistics and value chain optimization.
Agribusiness Feasibilty Study -
Trains students to develop investment-ready and technology-based agribusiness projects.
Entrepreneurship -
Builds a strong digital agripreneurial mindset and startup capability.
These five courses strongly represent the program's strength in technology integration, sustainability, value chain management, and entrepreneurial orientation.
Concentrations
Agritourism & Food Technology -
This concentration integrates agricultural production with tourism development and food processing innovation. Students learn value-added product development, agro-based tourism management, and post-harvest technology to enhance competitiveness and sustainability.
Smart Agri Innovation -
This concentration emphasizes digital agriculture, smart farming systems, precision agriculture, IoT applications, and data-driven agribusiness management. It prepares students to lead technology-based transformation in modern agriculture.
Elective Courses
For the Agritourism & Food Technology track, electives typically relate to agro-tourism management, food product development, post-harvest handling, and value-added processing. Meanwhile, under the Smart Agri Innovation track, elective courses focus on digital agriculture, precision farming, IoT applications, agribusiness data analytics, and innovation management.

Informatics

Informatics
About Us
The Informatics Study Program at President University is designed to prepare students to become industry-ready professionals in the digital era. Unlike conventional programs, Informatics at President University integrates academic learning with intensive bootcamp programs, ensuring that students gain not only strong theoretical foundations but also real-world technical experience through case-based projects from industry partners.
This program offers five future-oriented concentrations:
Artificial Intelligence, Internet of Things, Cyber Security,  Game Development and DevOps and Cloud Computing. Each concentration is directly aligned with global technology trends and is supported by hands-on bootcamps, collaborative team projects, and applied laboratories. Students regularly work on industry-relevant problems, participate in company visits, and engage in interdisciplinary projects that simulate professional environments.
In the third year, students enter a unique streaming system where they may choose one of four professional pathways:
International mobility programs, full-semester internships, startup incubation, or family business projects. This structure allows students to build portfolios, professional networks, and entrepreneurial capabilities even before graduation.
Three Key Facts About the Program
Internationally Accredited – The program is accredited by IABEE and holds an “Unggul” status from LAMINFOKOM, guaranteeing global academic standards and graduate quality.
English-Based Learning Environment – All courses are delivered fully in English, preparing graduates for international careers and multinational companies.
Industry-Integrated Curriculum – Learning is reinforced through bootcamps, industry visits, collaborative projects, and internship programs, ensuring that students master both hard skills and professional work culture.
Impact and Future Relevance
With rapid advancements in AI, cybersecurity threats, digital transformation, and smart systems, Informatics graduates are no longer just programmers—they are solution designers, system architects, and digital innovators. President University’s Informatics program equips students with adaptive skills, critical thinking, teamwork, and leadership abilities required in the Industry 4.0 and Society 5.0 era.
Graduates leave not only with a degree, but with certifications, project portfolios, industry experience, and global exposure, making this major a powerful investment for a sustainable and impactful future career.

Cyber Security Concentration: Cyber Security Fundamentals, Ethical Hacking, Cryptography and Data Security, Digital Forensics, Security Risk Management and Audit.
Artificial Intelligence Concentration: Deep Learning Fundamentals, Image Processing and Pattern Recognition, Natural Language Processing and Understanding, Computer Vision, Intelligent Robotics System.
Artificial Intelligence for Healthcare Concentration: Deep Learning Fundamentals, Medical Imaging and Computer Vision, Natural Language Processing in Healthcare, Data Science and Predictive Analytics in Healthcare, Virtual Reality (VR) and IoT (Wearable Devices) in Healthcare.
DevOps and Cloud Computing Concentration: Cloud Computing Fundamentals, DevOps Principles and Workflow Automation, Containerization and Orchestration, CI/CD Pipeline Implementation, Monitoring, Logging and Cloud Security.

Portfolio-Based Curriculum with a Bootcamp Model

An ERP concentration delivered in the form of a bootcamp, utilizing a full-license U.S.-based ERP system (Epicor), with 130 licenses valued at approximately 7 billion rupiah.

A Data Science Concentration delivered through a bootcamp model, with a strong focus on the implementation of data analytics and artificial intelligence in manufacturing and other industries.
The program involves industry practitioners and real-world cases from multinational companies across major industrial zones, such as PT Kalbe, PT Mattel Indonesia, and others.

Mechanical Engineering
About Us
The Mechanical Engineering Program at President University is designed to produce industry-ready engineers through a strong integration of theory and practice. Key distinguishing features include:
Integrated Project-Based Curriculum
Students learn through hands-on projects that reflect real engineering challenges, fostering critical thinking and innovation.
Collaborative Industry Engagement
The program maintains close partnerships with leading industries, ensuring curriculum relevance and real-world exposure.
Strong Emphasis on Professional Skills
Communication, teamwork, leadership, and ethics are embedded throughout the learning process.
Industry Visits and Applied Exposure
Regular site visits are practical learning experiences allow students to understand industrial environments firsthand.
Structured Internship Program
Students gain valuable professional experience through mandatory internships aligned with their academic specialization.
Three Key Facts About  Mechanical Engineering at President University
Broad Technical Scope
Students develop strong foundations across core mechanical engineering areas, enabling adaptability in diverse engineering roles.
Cross-Industry Applicability
Graduates are prepared to work in manufacturing energy, transportation, healthcare, automation, and many other sectors.
Problem-Solving Oriented
The curriculum emphasizes analytical thinking and practical problem-solving to address engineering challenges.
The Relevance of Mechanical Engineering Today
Mechanical Engineering remains a foundational discipline that drives modern technological advancement. It plays a critical role in:
Industrial transformation and smart manufacturing.
Energy systems and sustainable solutions.
Automation, robotics, and AI-assisted machinery.
Transportation systems and infrastructure development.
Real World Impact of Mechanical Engineering
Mechanical engineers contribute directly to solving global challenges by:
Improving renewable energy systems through advanced turbine and HVAC designs.
Advancing mobility through efficient engines and electric drive technologies.
Enhancing healthcare through precision mechanical devices and systems.
Optimizing manufacturing processes to reduce waste and improve quality.
By equipping students with strong practical and analytical capabilities, the program empowers graduates to create innovations with measurable economic, environmental, and social impact.
Future Opportunities for Mechanical Engineering Graduates
The Mechanical Engineering program at President University prepares students for emerging technologies and future industry demands, including:
Industry 4.0. and Smart Factories
— integration of cyber-physical systems and IoT in manufacturing
Robotics and Automation
— development of autonomous and collaborative robotic systems
Sustainable Engineering Solutions
— environmentally responsible system design
Advanced Materials and Additive Manufacturing (3D Printing)

Let us share our aspirations as the Faculty strives to fulfill the vision to be at the forefront in producing graduates who excel in academics, professions, and entrepreneurship, and contribute to the progress of Indonesian society and the world.
Dr.Eng. Lydia Anggraini, S.T., M.Eng.
Head Of Study Program
Welcome to the Mechanical Engineering Study Program -  a place where innovation meets real-world impact.
As Head of the Mechanical Engineering Program, I am proud to welcome both our current students and those considering joining our academic community. Our program is designed to prepare future engineers who are not only technically strong, but also creative, adaptive, and socially responsible.
What makes our program unique is our strong emphasis on hands-on learning, industry collaboration, and problem-based projects that reflect real engineering challenges. From industrial integrated laboratories and modern manufacturing facilities to interdisciplinary research and international opportunities, our students gain practical experience that goes far beyond the classroom.
We cultivate a learning environment that encourages curiosity, teamwork, and innovation, guided by dedicated faculty with strong academic and industry backgrounds.
We invite you to explore, create, and grow with us as we shape the engineers who will build a better and more sustainable future.
Welcome to Mechanical Engineering - where your ideas take motion.

Industrial Engineering
About Us
WHY Industrial Engineering President University?
The Industrial Engineering Program at President University is designed with a strong emphasis on Problem-Based Learning (PBL) and Project-Based Learning (PjBL), equipping students with practical, real-world problem-solving skills.
The curriculum is carefully tailored to meet industry demands by integrating distinctive components such as a Data Science Bootcamp, Integrated Mini Company Projects, and a one-semester internship in multinational companies.
Through these comprehensive learning approaches, graduates are not only technically proficient but also agile and critical thinkers—well-prepared to lead innovation in both manufacturing and service industries.
Duration
3-4 years
Degree
Sarjana Teknik (S.T.)
Accreditation
Unggul (LAMTEKNIK)
Curriculum Framework
Year
Subject
1
Basic Engineering Foundation
Students focus on mathematics, physics, computing, and an introduction to engineering concepts to build and strengthen fundamental technical skill
2
Analytical & Systematic Thinking Development
Students develop supporting knowledge in operations research, systems thinking, and decision-making. Critical and analytical thinking skills are enhanced through real-case projects and simulations.
3
Industrial Engineering Specialization & Application
Students master key Industrial Engineering disciplines such as supply chain management, quality control, process optimization, and production systems. The program culminates in capstone projects and a one-semester internship, where students solve real industrial problems.

Environmental Engineering
About Us
About Us
The Environmental Engineering program focuses on industrial waste management and industrial environmental management and is taught by lecturers with solid industry experience. It is the only program of its kind located in Indonesia’s largest industrial area. With the campus’s strategic location, it has developed strong relationships with nearby industrial companies in the field over the years. This allows students to easily pursue internships during their studies and start their careers upon graduating.
Vision and Mission
VISION
In 2030 will be an excellent Environmental Engineering Study Program nationally, in the field of Environmental Management and Industrial Waste by innovative approaches, global views, and valuable for the society
MISSION
To develop education system in Environmental Engineering field based on strong scientific knowledge, global views, and contribute actively in developing scientific knowledge that relates.
To create high-quality graduates in Environmental Engineering field, especially in Industrial Waste Management and Environmental Management.
To actively develop applied and innovative technology, and to promote inter-discipline cooperation and networking with industries, government, and other institutions in environmental engineering field.
To perform community service in environmental engineering field which will be oriented on innovation, problems solving and infrastructure development by considering the harmonious relationship between man and nature.

3-4 years
Degree

Accreditation
Unggul (LAMTEKNIK)

Core Courses
Unit Process, Unit Operation, Environmental Laboratory, Sustainable Water Network System, Environmental Impact Assessment, Material Flow Analysis, Water Treatment Design, Cost Estimation for Environmental Projects, Solid and Hazardous Waste Management, Occupational Health, Safety, and Environment, Wastewater Treatment Design, Air Pollution Control and Technology, Climate Change Adaptation and Mitigation, Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing; Pricing and Trading for Environmental Projects, Environmental Project Management, Environmental Engineering Capstone Design.
Concentration Courses
Carbon Accounting and Management, Eco-Industrial Development, Industrial Resources Recycling and Recovery, Health, Safety, and Sanitation.
Elective Courses
Eco-Industrial Park and Management, Environmental Circular Economy, Advanced Waste Treatment and Technology, Eco-Friendly Energy Sources, Origin and Characterization of Environmental Pollution, Industrial Hygiene.

International Relations
