International Relations
About Us
President University’s International Relations program produces "The Global Strategist"—a professional who combines the vision of a statesman, the precision of an analyst, with the speed of an entrepreneur.
We don’t just teach IR — we shape leaders.
Move Fast Advantage:
We offer an optimized 3-Year pathway to complete 144 SKS, allowing high-achievers to enter the workforce or postgraduate studies 1.5 years ahead of traditional undergraduate and even International Undergraduate Program peers.
Jababeka as the ‘Living Laboratory’:
Direct immersion in Southeast Asia’s largest industrial hub with 2,000+ MNCs, where students engage in Year 1 business projects and corporate diplomacy in a truly international community. This is IR education embedded in industry, not isolated in classrooms.
Elite Access & Global Immersion:
President University IR transforms global affairs into a daily professional reality through weekly briefings with Foreign Ambassadors, UN Directors, and executives from top-tier MNCs, offering elite mentorship that transcends traditional textbooks. This immersive experience is powered by a 30-nation campus community and a third-year Global Mobility Program, ensuring students master intercultural diplomacy in a real-world "Mini-UN" environment before they even graduate.
Operational Statesmanship:
We replace passive learning with
Professional Toolkits
, including specialized training in Sustainability (ESG) Reporting, Country Risk Analysis, Policy Brief Writing, and Strategic Negotiation.
Beyond "English-Only,"
A foundational 2-semester package in Mandarin, Japanese, French, or Spanish, with an optional Professional Certification Semester to secure globally recognized credentials (HSK, JLPT, DELF).
Capstone Project:
Your final project is a professional result, not just a thesis. Whether launching a social venture or conducting a risk audit for an embassy or MNC, you graduate with a Portfolio of Outcomes that ensures you are "Recruited Before Graduation.”
Duration
3 - 4 years
Degree
Sarjana Sosial (S.Sos.)
Accreditation
B (BAN PT)
Curriculum Framework
Core Courses
Theory, Foundations & Research Methods
This cluster oversees students' academic rigor and methodological training.Foundation & Theory: Foundation in Social Sciences, Introduction to Political Science, Introduction to International Relations, and IR Theories.Methodology: Statistics and Data Literacy and IR Research Methods.Academic Writing: First-Year Writing Seminar and Academic Journal Writing and Publishing.
Diplomacy & Global Governance
This cluster focuses on the practice of statecraft and the role of international institutions.
Core Practice: Global Leadership, Diplomacy and Ambassadorial Practice, and IR Seminar.Institutions & Law: Principles of International Law and International Organizations, Global Governance and the UN.Policy & Analysis: Foreign Policy Analysis and Indonesian Foreign and Security Policy.Professional Skills: Policy Brief Writing, Negotiation Technique and Lobbying, and Diplomatic Protocol.
Security, Strategy & Conflict Studies
This cluster explores how states and non-state actors use various strategies— from military power to diplomacy and intelligence — to manage, resolve, or engage in conflicts.
Core Security: International Security.
Thematic Electives: Study of War and Peace, Defense Policy, Analysis of Strategic Domain and Seapower and Maritime Affairs.
Modern Threats: Security Revolution and Cyber Security and Conflict Studies.
International Political Economy (IPE) & Corporate Diplomacy
This Cluster bridges the gap between international politics, business, and economics.Core Economics: International Political Economy and Economic Survival (Gen-AI/Business Project).Corporate Track: Corporate Diplomacy, Business in World Politics, and the Corporate Diplomacy Bootcamp.Trade & Development: the Politics of International Trade and International Development Cooperation in Practice.
Area & Regional Studies
This cluster focuses on specific geographic and political landscapes.Regional Core: The Dynamics of ASEAN and Comparative Politics.Regional Electives: Politics of Europe and the EU, Domestic and Foreign Affairs of China, US Foreign Policy, and International Relations and Development in Africa.Specialized Areas:AreasDynamics of the MENA Region, East Asia in the World, and Discover Palestine.
Global Issues & Sustainability (Transnational Studies)
This cluster handles cross-cutting themes that affect the global community beyond state borders.Human Rights & Society: Human Rights and Human Security, Gender Studies, and International Migration.Environment: Environmental Issues in IR and Sustainable Development/Global Environmental Politics.Culture & Soft Power: International Culture and Communication, Popular Culture and Globalization, and Gastrodiplomacy.
Concentration Courses
Global Policy
Policy Brief Writing, Negotiation Technique and Lobbying, and Diplomatic Protocol and Etiquette.
International Development
Project Proposal and Concept Note Writing, and Sustainability Reporting.
Corporate Diplomacy
Country Risk, and Government Relations Skills.
Facilities
Diplomacy Laboratory
This lab is the primary training ground for the practical "hard skills" of international relations. It benefits IR students by providing a simulated environment to master the mechanics of statecraft—including public speaking, diplomatic protocol, crisis negotiation, and table-top exercises. This is where students physically practice the art of diplomacy through mock summits and role-play scenarios, ensuring they graduate with the confidence and technical competence required for real-world diplomatic missions and foreign ministry assignments.Crisis Negotiation - Ambassadorial Practice - Protocol Mastery - Mock Summits.
President  Center for International Studies (PRECIS)
PRECIS operates as the primary research think tank for the School of International Relations, specifically designed to cultivate students' academic and analytical rigor in strategic studies, global security, and foreign policy. For IR students, this facility is invaluable as a launchpad for scholarly publication and policy research, offering opportunities to work directly alongside senior experts on high-level projects ranging from maritime defense doctrines to counter-terrorism strategies. By engaging with PRECIS, students transition from passive learners to active researchers, gaining privileged access to assistantships, mentorship for publishing in reputable journals like AEGIS, and the critical analytical skills required for careers in intelligence, government analysis, and academia.Research hub for strategic studies - security analysis - foreign policy research.
President Center for UN Studies
This facility functions as a specialized academic hub dedicated to the research and promotion of the United Nations' core values, specifically the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Unlike a simulation lab, this center benefits IR students by offering a rigorous academic environment for policy analysis and deep-dive research into global governance challenges. It provides students with opportunities to engage in high-level discourse on human rights, peace-building, and international development, preparing them for careers as policy advisors, UN staff members, or researchers who need a profound theoretical understanding of the UN system rather than just procedural mechanics.SDGs - Human Rights - Peace-building.
Indonesia-China Partnership Institution (ICPI-PU)
The ICPI-PU functions as a specialized strategic hub that allows IR students to examine the geopolitical and economic intricacies of one of the Asia-Pacific's most critical bilateral relationships. For aspiring diplomats and analysts, this institution offers a front-row seat to the dynamics of Sino-Indonesian cooperation, ranging from trade policy to cultural soft power. The facility benefits students by cultivating deep regional expertise and cross-cultural competence, ensuring they are competitive candidates for careers involving East Asian foreign policy, international trade, and transnational corporate relations.Sino-Indonesian relations - trade diplomacy - cultural soft power.
Adam Kurniawan Library
Supports academic and research activities by providing access to books, academic journals, and digital resources relevant to international relations and related disciplines.
Setsail BizAccel
Setsail BizAccel is a business accelerator program at President University, Indonesia, aimed at fostering an entrepreneurship ecosystem among students. The program includes initiatives such as the "Ready Set Sail" business incubation program and the "SetSail Startup Bootcamp."Social ventures - Startups - Innovation Project.

Business Development Strategist
Market Analyst

Dr. Mohammad Syafi'i Anwar
Dean of The Faculty of Social Science and Education
“As a Dean of Faculty of Social Science and Education, President University, I am committed to enhance the quality of all students of Department of international Relations, Law, Communication, and Education to be well-qualified students who have global mindset, entrepreneurship spirit, and ready to show their international standard performance.”
Muhammad Sigit Andhi Rahman, Ph.D.
Head Of Study Program
Welcome to the Future of Diplomacy: Bridging the Gap Between Classroom and Boardroom
Welcome to the International Relations (IR) Study Program at President University, where we don’t just teach you to read the global map—we prepare you to build the bridges that connect it. In today's volatile global economy, diplomacy has moved beyond the halls of government to become the very heartbeat of international business. Our mission is to forge "The Global Strategist"—a professional who combines the vision of a statesman, the precision of an analyst, with the speed of an entrepreneur.
At President University, we operate on the DNA of our founder, SD Darmono: Think Big, Start Small, and Move Fast. This philosophy is embedded in our optimized 3-year "Fast Track" pathway, which allows high-achieving students to complete all 144 SKS and enter the global workforce or prestigious postgraduate studies 1.5 years earlier than their peers at other institutions. We believe that time is your greatest asset, and our curriculum is designed to respect and maximize it. Move fast. Lead early.
Your learning journey here takes place in the "Living Laboratory" of Jababeka, Southeast Asia’s largest industrial hub. Surrounded by over 2,000 multinational corporations, you will engage in real-world corporate diplomacy and business projects from your very first year.  Study IR inside a real global economy.
We replace passive learning with "Operational Statesmanship," providing you with professional toolkits in Sustainability (ESG) Reporting, Country Risk Analysis, and Policy Brief writing. Furthermore, our 2+1 Language Stack ensures you graduate not only with English fluency but also with professional certifications in Mandarin, Japanese, French, or Spanish, securing your place in any international boardroom.
Global immersion is our daily reality. You will experience direct mentorship through our Weekly Ambassadorial & Global Leader Series, learning statecraft from sitting foreign ambassadors, UN directors, and Fortune 500 executives. This elite access, combined with a hyper-diverse campus representing over 30 nationalities, creates a "Mini-UN" environment that naturally sharpens your intercultural negotiation skills. By your third year, you can choose to take your studies worldwide through our Global Mobility Program or pursue high-impact internships at embassies and international organizations.
We do not just want students who can define diplomacy; we want students who can execute it. Your time here will culminate in a Capstone Project focused on "Problem Ownership"—a tangible result, such as launching a social venture or conducting a risk audit for an MNC—that forms part of a portfolio of outcomes that will help you get recruited before graduation.
I invite you to join this elite community of global strategists and start building your ship while you sail. Welcome to the beginning of your global career.
Lead globally. Act strategically. Move fast.
Become The Global Strategist.
M. Sigit Andhi Rahman, S.I.P., M.A., Ph.D.
Head of International Relations Study Program President University

Communication
About Us
This program is designed to equip students with both foundational knowledge and specialized skills across three dynamic concentrations: Public Relations & Digital Communication, Film and Media Studies, and Content Creation. It stands out for its hands-on, industry-integrated learning model, preparing students not just for today’s media landscape but for the future of communication.
The major’s unique features:
1. Blended Learning of Theory and Practice
From Year 1, students engage in real-world projects such as:
Economic Survival 1 & 2: Combining Gen-AI tools, internship experience, and business projects.
Bootcamps: News Writing (Bootcamp 1) and Digital Public Relations (Bootcamp 2) provide intensive, skill-based training.
Social Project for Communication in the short semester, emphasizing community and social impact.
2. Three Future-Focused Concentrations
Students can specialize in:
Public Relations & Digital Communication (Crisis Communication, Digital PR, Event Management)
Film and Media Studies (Film-based and Mass Media skills such as Film Directing, Film Scenario, Convergence News Media Production and Reporting)
Content Creation (platform-specific content for TikTok, Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube, and corporate social media labs)
3. Strong Emphasis on Research and Strategy
Courses like Quantitative & Qualitative Communication Research Methods and Statistics for Communication ensure students can analyze trends, measure impact, and create data-driven communication strategies.
Why This Major Is Relevant, Impactful, and Future-Oriented?
·
Relevance:
The curriculum directly addresses digital transformation in communication, with courses in digital PR, social media content creation, and multimedia storytelling. It aligns with industry needs for versatile communicators who can work across platforms.
·
Impactful
: Through collaborative projects, industry visits (implied in bootcamps and labs), and social projects, students learn to create communication that drives social and business impact. The Final Project and Social Project ensure tangible outcomes.
·
Future-Oriented
: The program integrates AI tools (Gen-Al), digital content labs, and international mobility options, preparing students for global and tech-driven communication careers. The internship/work-integrated learning in Year 3 bridges academic learning with professional experience.
·
Hands-On and Collaborative Elements Included
·
Internship Programs
: Multiple options in Year 3, including startups, family businesses, international mobility, and entrepreneurship paths.
·
Industry Collaboration
: Bootcamps, corporate client projects, and the PresUniv Content Creation Labs offer direct industry engagement.
·
Collaborative Projects:
Group-based projects in content creation, event management, and social campaigns foster teamwork and practical skills.

3.5 - 4 year

Elementary Teacher Education
Elementary Teacher Education
About Us
Instead of recruiting people from Elementary Teacher Education programs, it is practical for international elementary schools in Indonesia to recruit their teachers from English Education program, as English is used as the primary medium of instructions.
To address this gap, the Elementary Teacher Education (ETE) students in President University are equipped with the competencies to teach in international elementary schools using English as the medium of instruction.
The ETE curriculum is designed to prepare future educators with a strong foundation in international curricula, including the International Baccalaureate (IB), Cambridge, and other globally recognized educational frameworks, ensuring graduates are competitive, confident, and ready to teach in international learning environments.

President University’s Elementary Teacher Education program is one of the best in Indonesia. The program received an “Excellent” accreditation from LAMDIK in 2024. Our curriculum prepares students to become teachers in international schools. Most of our alumni work in international schools and actively support current students by providing internship opportunities at their institutions.
Approximately one quarter of our students are international students from China, Myanmar, and Brunei, creating a truly international learning environment.

About Us
Why LAW at President University?
Our curriculum is well adapted with the trend of legal profession. This is reflected by various courses that prepare graduates to have a legal career that is needed by society.
We teach practical knowledge to our students very early through our Legal Clinic courses where they are trained to implement legal theory to real-life case studies.
We collaborate with industry leaders to allow internships, visits, and guest lecture sessions to enrich the students’ knowledge with most-updated legal issues.

Welcome to the Faculty of Medicine, President University. Located in the heart of Indonesia’s largest industrial and economic hub, our faculty offers a dynamic, innovative, and globally oriented medical education environment. From Bekasi to Indonesia and the world, your journey to becoming a future-ready doctor begins here.

If you aspire to be a doctor who serves the community, competes globally, and leads the future of healthcare, this is where your journey begins.

Interior Design
Architecture
Visual Communication Design
Interior Design
About Us
Why Interior Design at President University?
Many people think interior design is just about choosing colors, furniture, or making spaces “pretty.” In reality, interior design is about how people experience space, how comfortable they feel, how they move, how they work, how they interact, and how a space supports their well-being, safety, and activities.
Our program is designed around this deeper understanding of space and people.
1.Progressive, studio-based learning from homes to public spaces
You don’t start with complicated buildings on day one. You begin with spaces people already understand: homes. In early studios, students learn how to design residential interiors such as bedrooms, living rooms, kitchens focusing on:
how people live,
how furniture works,
how light, air, and materials affect comfort, and
how layout influences daily life.
As students progress, they move to more complex spaces, such as:
retail stores,
hotels and mixed-use buildings, and
public spaces.
This means learning grows step by step, just like real professional practice. By the end of the program, students are able to design spaces that serve many people, not just one family but also community areas, commercial spaces, or hospitality environments.
2. Strong industry alignment , more than decoration
Interior designers today must understand much more than style. Our curriculum connects design with real industry needs, including:
Sustainability — how to design spaces that save energy, reduce waste, and use responsible materials.
Building systems — understanding lighting, ventilation, acoustics, and how buildings function so designs are safe and comfortable, not just beautiful.
Professional practice — how real projects work: budgets, timelines, teamwork, communication with clients, and collaboration with architects and contractors.
In simple terms: students learn how to design spaces that are beautiful, functional, safe, and responsible, these are the same expectations found in real design firms.
Duration
3-4 years
Degree
Sarjana Desain (S.Ds.)
Accreditation
Baik (BAN PT)
Curriculum Framework
Semester
Subject
1

2
Economic Survival 2: Gen-AI and Internship Experience/Business Project for Art, Design, and Architecture, Interior Design Studio 1: Private-Commercial Residential Design, Color and Light, History of Eastern Interior Design, Basic Design Principles (3D), Professional Practices in Design.
Short Semester 1
Economic Survival 3: Social Project for Art, Design, and Architecture, Religion, Pancasila, Citizenship, Indonesian Language
3
Interior Design Studio 2: Retail Space Design for Tourism Industry, Furniture Design 1, Building Physics, Visual Merchandising, Elective 1, Elective 2.
4

Short Semester 2
Elective 5, Elective 6, Elective 7
5
Work-Integrated Learning, Interior Design Studio 4: Public Space Design Specialization Projects, Design for Tourism, Building Codes & Regulation, Project Management, Project Management, Elective 8, Elective 9.
6
Option 1 – Internship 1*, Option 2 – Internship at Startup or Family Owned Business 1*, Option 3 – Entrepreneurship/Family Business 1*, Option 4 – International Student Mobility 1*, Option 5 – Scholars 1*
Short Semester 3
Elective 10, Project Proposal, Final Project

144

Architecture
About Us
Fast Career Readiness through a Full-Semester Internship
The Architecture program at President University integrates a mandatory one-semester internship, allowing students to gain real professional experience before graduation. This structure significantly accelerates career readiness, and many students secure employment shortly after—or even before—graduation.
English-Speaking and International Learning Environment
All courses are delivered in English, creating an international academic atmosphere that prepares students for global collaboration. Daily academic discussions, presentations, and studio critiques in English strengthen communication skills that are essential in today’s international architectural practice.
Strong Exposure to Real Practice through Site and Company Visits
Every design studio (laboratory) is supported by regular site visits, ensuring that design decisions are grounded in real spatial, social, and environmental contexts. In addition, company visits are organized every semester, exposing students to architectural firms, construction companies, and related industries. This hands-on approach helps students understand how architects work in real professional settings—while also making learning dynamic, exploratory, and enjoyable.

Visual Communication Design
About Us
Visual Communication Design at President University uniquely combines strong design fundamentals, emerging media, and Nusantara visual culture, preparing students for global creative industries.
3 key facts:
It is the one from a few VCD programs in Indonesia providing full, free Adobe Creative Cloud access until graduation for each student.
Students choose two strong concentrations: Graphic Design & Emerging Media or Animation & Game.
The program emphasizes small classes (25 students) for intensive mentoring and collaboration.
This major is relevant and future-oriented because it integrates digital technology, new media, and cultural preservation while responding to industry needs. Students gain real-world experience through collaborative projects, industry & museum visits, social projects, exhibitions, and a structured internship program, ensuring strong professional readiness.

Master of Management in Technology
About Us
WHY Master of Management in Technology President University?
This program offers two clearly structured academic pathways:
By Course, designed for professionals seeking advanced applied skills and industry-ready expertise.
By Research, intended for students who aim to become researchers, lecturers, or pursue a PhD.
Research-Driven yet Application-Oriented
Unlike conventional programs that focus solely on theory or implementation, this program integrates:
Advanced informatics research (AI, Data Science, NLP, Cybersecurity)
Real-world problem solving through applied projects and case studies
Future-Oriented Curriculum
The curriculum is designed to address current and emerging digital challenges, including:
Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning
Big Data & Intelligent Systems
Cloud Computing & Cyber Security
Advanced Computing & Data Analytics
A flexible, research-integrated Master of Informatics program that combines advanced technology, real-world application, and future-oriented innovation.

Doctor of Management
Doctor of Management
About Us
WHY Doctoral Program in Management at President University?
The Doctoral Program in Management at President University is uniquely designed to develop graduates who are capable of leading and applying strategic business transformation through the integration of rigorous doctoral-level research, global and multicultural perspectives, and advanced managerial practice. The program combines research excellence with business and policy relevance, enabling graduates to translate original research findings into evidence-based strategies, organizational transformation initiatives, and sustainable value creation. Through its four clearly structured concentrations—Marketing and Innovation; Strategic Management and Human Capital; Sustainable Finance and Global Operations; and Management Information Systems, the program equips graduates with deep analytical, digital, financial, and organizational capabilities necessary to diagnose complex problems, design transformation roadmaps, and implement strategic change in dynamic global environments. Embedded within an outcome-based education framework and supported by measurable research and professional outputs, the program ensures that graduates emerge not only as scholars but also as transformational leaders who can drive innovation, sustainability, and competitive advantage across industries and international contexts.

Welcome message from the Head of Management Study Program – Doctoral Program, Faculty of Business, President University: Dr. Stephanus Remond Waworuntu, M.B.A.
Lead the Future of Business Transformation
Welcome to Indonesia’s pioneer full English-medium Doctor in Management Program. We offer a prestigious academic pathway, meticulously designed for ambitious professionals to earn their Doctorate within a streamlined three-year timeframe. This program is specifically tailored for academics, researchers, consultants, and corporate executives poised to orchestrate strategic business transformations through four robustly structured concentrations:
Marketing and InnovationStrategic Management and Human CapitalSustainable Finance and Global OperationsManagement Information Systems
Our vision is to become a preeminent, internationally recognized doctoral program, distinguished by Research Excellence. We are dedicated to developing academic and professional leaders characterized by a global mindset, multicultural competence, and an entrepreneurial spirit—individuals capable of making significant strategic contributions to the advancement of knowledge, business practices, and society at large.
Are you ready to ascend to the pinnacle of your academic and professional career? Join us and take your place among the next generation of internationally acclaimed leaders.
Join us and become part of the next generation of global leaders.

Fast Track AI Convergence
Fast Track AI Convergence - Indonesia’s First Integrated AI + Discipline Program – Bachelor’s + Master’s in Just Over 4 Years
Fast Track AI Convergence is the first program in Indonesia to offer an integrated pathway that blends your major of choice with cutting-edge Artificial Intelligence – from Day 1 of your undergraduate journey to graduation with a Master’s degree.
In just over 4 years, students complete their Bachelor’s and Master’s degrees, developing both domain expertise and future-focused tech mastery.
Integrated Pathway: Earn both a Bachelor’s and Master’s degree in just over 4 years
Core AI Foundations: Machine learning, data science, AI ethics, and automation
Interdisciplinary Focus: Combine AI with Engineering, Business, Design, Law, or Social Science
Hands-On Learning: Real-world projects, internships, and cross-disciplinary collaboration
Capstone Requirement: Complete a thesis or practical project that solves a real-world problem
Ethical Innovation: Emphasis on responsible AI design and application
Career-Ready Graduates: Equipped to lead in tech-driven industries or public sectors

PUSH
President University Student Housing (PUSH) is beyond dormitory. It is an environment that supports students from all over the world to grow academically, socially, and personally. The combination of structured programs, complete facilities, and a supportive community helps students prepare for future challenges and responsibilities.
Overview
President University Student Housing (PUSH) provides a structured and supportive living environment for students. It combines accommodation with programs that strengthen academic progress, leadership skills, and social development. PUSH consists of several residential sites, including
SBH (Student Boarding House)
and
NBH (New Beverly Hills)
.
NBH offers both single and shared rooms, while SBH provides shared rooms only. Each sharing room accommodates a maximum of two residents, which supports both privacy and interaction. The environment encourages daily engagement among students from different cultural and academic backgrounds.
Vision
PUSH aims to become an excellent, safe, and inclusive student residence. The environment supports academic achievement, leadership growth, and the development of a global mindset. All activities and systems are built on values of togetherness, diversity, and sustainability.
Mission
PUSH provides safe and comfortable housing supported by complete facilities and an integrated security system, which ensures students feel secure in their daily activities.
PUSH develops leadership and soft skills through a structured Dormitory Curriculum that runs throughout the academic year.
PUSH builds a multicultural community where students practice tolerance, respect, and ethical behavior in daily interactions.
PUSH supports academic and professional growth by encouraging networking and preparing students for future careers.
PUSH promotes social responsibility and environmental awareness through community service and sustainability programs.
PUSH supports physical and mental well-being through sports activities, access to health services, and a balanced living environment.
Objectives
PUSH helps new students adapt to university life through a structured and supportive residential system.
PUSH builds leadership character and encourages responsibility through active participation in programs and activities.
PUSH expands student networks, both socially and professionally.
PUSH creates a respectful and inclusive environment across different cultures and religions.
PUSH improves career readiness through resume development and professional profile building such as LinkedIn.
PUSH promotes healthy living through sports, wellness programs, and available facilities.
PUSH develops environmental awareness through consistent implementation of Go Green programs.
PUSH strengthens relationships and a sense of belonging among residents.
Residential Sites and Room Options
SBH (Student Boarding House) provides shared rooms only. Each room accommodates up to two students, which encourages interaction, teamwork, and shared responsibility in daily living.
NBH (New Beverly Hills) provides both single rooms and shared rooms. Single rooms support privacy and independent living, while shared rooms accommodate up to two students and encourage social interaction. This variety allows students to choose based on their preferences and needs.
Programs and Activities
a. Dormitory Curriculum
The Dormitory Curriculum is a core program that runs continuously across Semester 1, Semester 2, and Semester 3. It focuses on practical skill development and prepares students for both academic and professional environments. Through social projects, students gain experience in teamwork and community engagement. Leadership activities help students build confidence, decision-making skills, and responsibility. Networking trains students to build meaningful connections that support their future careers. In addition, resume development and LinkedIn updates guide students in presenting their experiences and achievements in a professional way. This program ensures students not only learn academically but also develop essential life and career skills.
b. Community and Engagement
Bonding Night (Makrab) serves as a bonding program that brings residents together through shared activities. It helps students build friendships, improve communication, and create a sense of belonging within the dormitory environment.
c. Cultural and Religious Activities
Interfaith prayer during Ramadan provides a space for students to practice respect and tolerance across different religions. The Chinese New Year celebration introduces cultural values and traditions, which helps students understand and appreciate diversity in a practical way. In addition, other cultural and religious activities are accommodated in PUSH.
d. Environmental Program
The Go Green program encourages students to take part in environmental activities such as planting, waste management, and daily eco-friendly practices. These activities build awareness and responsibility toward sustainability.
e. Sports and Wellness
PUSH Cup is a sports competition that encourages physical activity, teamwork, and discipline. It also strengthens relationships among residents through healthy competition.
Development Strategies
a. Leadership and Character Building
Leadership camps and social projects are organized to strengthen responsibility, teamwork, and initiative among students.
b. Multicultural Engagement
Programs are designed to encourage interaction among students from different cultural and national backgrounds, which builds understanding and respect.
c. Health and Safety
PUSH provides access to sports facilities such as basketball and volleyball courts to support physical activity. Health services are available through the President University Medical Center. A 24-hour security system with CCTV ensures a safe living environment.
d. Sustainability
Go Green programs run continuously and include both practical activities and education to build long-term environmental awareness.
e. Student Engagement
PUSH uses the PUIS system as a platform for communication, feedback, and evaluation. Regular social and recreational activities are organized to maintain active student participation.
Facilities
PUSH provides facilities that support daily student needs and comfort. These include sports courts for basketball and volleyball, a prayer room, gazebo, drinking water stations, canteen, parking area, Wi-Fi access, electricity token system, 24-hour security, PUIS communication system, and Popbox for package storage. These facilities support both daily living and student activities.

Swimming Pool
With semi international standard, our swimming pool can occupied up to150 people.
The Swimming Pool at Jababeka Country Club is one of the sports facilities located in the Cikarang area. The pool meets semi-international standards and can accommodate up to 150 people. This makes it not only a great spot for weekend leisure but also a frequent training venue for athletes preparing for competitions.
The swimming pool at Jababeka Golf & Country Club is open to all age groups—from children and teenagers to adults and seniors. Visitors need not worry, as the facility is equipped with adequate safety systems to ensure everyone's security. Enhanced by a modern architectural design blended with natural elements, the pool offers a refreshing and enjoyable swimming experience.
While enjoying the sports facilities at the Swimming Pool of Jababeka Country Club, visitors can feel a strong connection to the surrounding environment. It’s no surprise that this pool is a favorite destination for both recreation and training, thanks to the comfort it provides. The atmosphere is ideal for relaxing and exercising—an excellent escape from the stress of daily routines.

Spacious parking area
Restaurant with a wide selection of menu items
Quiet prayer room
Clean restrooms
Wi-Fi to support connectivity needs

Outdoor Sports Field
Designed by Nick Faldo, Jababeka Golf & Country Club features an 18-hole course with full facilities, including a driving range, clubhouse, and event spaces for up to 200 guests.
Jababeka Golf & Country Club, surrounded by lavish trees with magnificent shrubs and lush green fields, offers you the best backdrop for your business and recreation experiences.
Designed by world-class golfer and architect Nick Faldo, Jababeka Golf and Country Club is just 30 minutes east of Jakarta with a lush green landscape in a region of exceptional serenity and exquisiteness. A masterful layout of 18 holes, Jababeka Golf Course and Country Club is truly a sight to behold, as it provides challenges and enjoyment to players of all skill levels — from beginner to professional — through the use of water features and special topography.
The course holds the vision of its architect, where the design benefits from the same meticulous finishes found in Faldo’s playing method. It can be as challenging or as friendly as you like, through the wide choice of tees and the large variety of pin positions.
A truly comprehensive facility, Jababeka Golf and Country Club features a golf pro-shop that carries the finest names in golf equipment and apparel, male and female locker rooms, and a restaurant that offers a wide array of excellent cuisine.
There is also a full-service driving range, putting greens, a well-maintained swimming pool, two quality tennis courts, a children’s playground, massage service, and VIP and function rooms that can be tailored to your needs — whether for a sophisticated business meeting or a private party.

Adam Kurniawan Library
Adam Kurniawan Library at President University is a modern, accredited library supporting academic research with extensive print and digital collections, study spaces, and a BI Corner.
The Adam Kurniawan Library at President University is a modern academic library named in honor of Adam Kurniawan, a respected entrepreneur and co-founder of the Jababeka Group. Established in 2003 and expanded in 2016, it serves as a central hub for learning and research, supporting the university's academic community.
Key Features:
Extensive Collections:
The library houses a wide range of materials, including textbooks, journals, fiction, student theses, and digital media. president.ac.id
Modern Facilities:
It offers comfortable study environments with general and individual study rooms, discussion areas, and free Wi-Fi to enhance the learning experience.
Digital Access:
An Online Public Access Catalog (OPAC) is available for users to search and access the library's resources remotely.
BI Corner:
In collaboration with Bank Indonesia, the library features a BI Corner, providing resources and information related to economics and finance.
Accreditation:
In March 2024, the library achieved an "A" rating from the National Library of the Republic of Indonesia, reflecting its excellence in services, collections, and facilities.

President Executive Club
President Executive Club is a premium business club and hotel in Jababeka for networking and hospitality.
President Executive Club (PEC) was founded by SD Darmono in June 2004 as a club for the meeting of minds and networking for President and Executives of 700 corporates in Cikarang Industrial Estate - Jababeka.
The groundbreaking for the PEC building in Cikarang CBD was by Susilo Bambang Yudhoyono (Indonesian presidential candidate for 2004 election), Edward Lee (the Ambassador of Singapore) and Setyono Djuandi Darmono, attended by distinguished businessmen and top executives.
In 2009, PEC was transformed to a national club with 11 founders, inaugurated by the Vice President at Ritz Carlton Jakarta.

Charles Himawan Auditorium
Charles Himawan Auditorium is a versatile event space at President University for seminars and student activities.
The Charles Himawan Auditorium is a prominent facility located on the 5th floor of Building A at President University’s campus in Kota Jababeka, Cikarang. Named after Prof. Dr. Charles Himawan, a distinguished legal scholar and one of the university's founding figures, the auditorium serves as a central venue for various academic and extracurricular activities.
This auditorium is equipped to host a range of events, including seminars, conferences, cultural performances, and student gatherings. For instance, on April 29, 2025, it was the venue for a seminar titled “Indonesia as a World Economic Power: Legal Perspective,” which featured discussions on digital innovation and economic sovereignty. Additionally, it has been used for events like table tennis tournaments, reflecting its versatility in accommodating both formal and recreational activities.
The Charles Himawan Auditorium stands as a testament to President University's commitment to providing spaces that foster intellectual growth, cultural exchange, and community engagement.

President University Convention Center
President University Convention Center is a modern venue for conferences, exhibitions, and large-scale events.
The President University Convention Center (PUCC) is a modern, multi-purpose venue located within the Jababeka Education Park, Cikarang. Designed to accommodate large-scale gatherings, PUCC serves as a key facility for conferences, exhibitions, graduations, and cultural events at President University.
Spanning two floors with a total area of approximately 1,900 m², PUCC can host up to 4,000 attendees in standing arrangements and around 3,600 in seated configurations. The center features a spacious ballroom with high ceilings, advanced air conditioning systems, and flexible staging options. On September 2024, PUCC was the venue for the Mandarin Career Expo, connecting students with international career opportunities, while in 2025 it hosted President University’s graduation ceremony for over 1,400 graduates.
The President University Convention Center reflects the university’s dedication to supporting academic excellence, professional development, and vibrant community engagement through world-class facilities.

The President University Tennis Court
The President University Tennis Court is a well-maintained sports facility designed to support student athletics, training, and recreational tennis activities on campus.
The President University Tennis Court is one of the sports facilities located within the campus area of President University in Kota Jababeka, Cikarang. Built to promote a healthy and active lifestyle among students, staff, and the surrounding community, the tennis court provides a dedicated space for both casual play and competitive training.
The court features a standard hard surface suitable for singles and doubles matches. Surrounded by protective fencing and equipped with proper lighting, the facility ensures safety and allows for extended playing hours, including in the late afternoon or early evening. The tennis court is often used for intramural tournaments, student club practices, and friendly matches between faculties and guests.
For example, in March 2024, the court hosted a friendly tennis match between the Faculty of Business and Faculty of Computing as part of the university's annual Sports Week, aiming to build camaraderie and sportsmanship among students. The facility also occasionally serves as a venue for regional community tennis programs.
The tennis court exemplifies President University’s commitment to providing sports amenities that enhance student well-being, teamwork, and extracurricular balance.

President University Lyrics Unplugged (PULU)
​President University Lyrics Unplugged (PULU) is a regular music and creative event organized by President University
Purpose and Impact
PULU is designed to bridge the gap between academic learning and real-world experiences in the creative industry. By featuring musicians, song-writers, and artists from various backgrounds, the event encourages students to explore their passions, develop their talents, and gain valuable insights into the music and entertainment sectors. It fosters a holistic educational environment where students are inspired to pursue excellence both within and beyond the classroom.

President University International Dinners (PUID)
President University International Dinner celebrates cultural diversity as international students share traditional dishes and stories.
Purpose and Impact
The President University International Dinners (PUID) is a vibrant cultural event where international students from various countries showcase their traditional cuisines. As a celebration of global diversity, the event invites students to prepare and present signature dishes from their home countries, offering a unique opportunity for cross-cultural exchange. The atmosphere is lively and festive, filled with the aromas and flavors of international dishes, as students from different backgrounds come together to taste, share stories, and learn about each other’s culinary heritage.
PUID serves as a platform for gastrodiplomacy, promoting mutual understanding and connection through food. By allowing students to experience the traditions and flavors of other nations firsthand, the event fosters unity, respect, and appreciation among the diverse university community. Not only does it highlight the importance of cultural identity through cuisine but also strengthens international friendship in an enjoyable and meaningful way.

The program fosters artistic expression, promotes campus culture, and gives students a professional platform to showcase their talents to peers, lecturers, and guests.

President University Nusantara Dinners (PUND)
President University Nusantara Dinners celebrates cultural diversity as Indonesian students share traditional dishes and stories.
Purpose and Impact
The President University Nusantara Dinners (PUND) is a vibrant cultural event that celebrates the rich diversity of Indonesia. Students from various regions across the country showcase their traditional cuisines, introducing authentic dishes that reflect their local heritage.
As a platform for cultural appreciation, the event invites students to prepare and present signature foods from their hometowns, creating an immersive experience of Indonesia’s culinary landscape. The atmosphere is lively and festive, filled with the distinctive aromas and flavors of regional specialties, as participants from all over the world come together to taste, share stories, and deepen their understanding of Indonesia’s diverse cultures.

Accounting Program at President Univ Collaborates with MNC Sekuritas and Avrist Asset Management for “Having Fund 2024” Event: The Benefits of Knowing Mutual Funds
October 29, 2025
On Tuesday, October 29, 2024, the Accounting Program from the Faculty of Business at President University (Presuniv) hosted the “Having Fund 2024” event in the Charles Himawan Auditorium, Building A, 5th Floor, at Presuniv’s campus in Kota Jababeka, Cikarang. This was a collaborative event between the Accounting Program, MNC Sekuritas, and Avrist Asset Management aimed to enhance students’ financial literacy under the theme The Benefits of Knowing Mutual Funds.
The event focused on introducing students to the importance of investment, particularly mutual funds, as a straightforward and secure choice for beginners. Representatives from MNC Sekuritas and Avrist Asset Management provided insights on long-term investment concepts, explaining a variety of instruments suited to individual needs and risk profiles.
The session featured two key speakers, Wesley Paraja Guguk, Senior Marketing APRD from MNC Sekuritas, and Tukul Rusyidi Khairi, Manager of Avrist Asset Management. Both speakers shared their knowledge and experience on mutual fund investments in a practical, and easy-to-understand manner. They also provided a step-by-step guide on starting investments with small amounts, encouraging students to manage their monthly savings wisely and incrementally.

Setsail BizAccel and International Relations Study Program, President University, Hold Sharing Session with Jonathan Davy, Co-Founder and CEO of Ecoxyztem
October 17, 2025
On Thursday, 17 October 2024, the International Relations Study Program of the Faculty of Social Sciences and Education, President University (Presuniv), in collaboration with Setsail BizAccel, a business incubator, organized a Sharing Sessions of International Relations Alumni featuring Jonathan Davy, Co-Founder and CEO of Ecoxyztem. The event was held at the Fabrication Laboratory (Fablab), President University Convention Center (PUCC), Jl. H. Usmar Ismail, Kota Jababeka, Cikarang, and focused on Jonathan’s experience in starting a business with his partners. Jonathan is an alumnus of the International Relations Program, class of 2011.
Dr. Mohammad Syafi’i Anwar, Dean of the Faculty of Social Sciences and Education expressed President University’s strong support for students who aspire to develop entrepreneurial skills and have the courage to start their own businesses. “One of the career paths offered by Presuniv is entrepreneurship, as Jonathan has pursued,” he emphasized. Meanwhile, Dr. Jeanne Francoise, Head of the International Relations Program, hoped that Jonathan’s presence would have a positive impact on all students. “I hope Jonathan’s presence will inspire students,” she remarked.
In his presentation, Jonathan shared that he originally wanted to become a diplomat. However, his dream was halted as there was a two-year moratorium on diplomat recruitment when he graduated from Presuniv. This situation forced him to change direction. Together with his friends, he decided to start his own business, leading to the establishment of Ecoxyztem.
Jonathan emphasized the importance of self-exploration during college. “Try to find out what you are passionate about. Once you find it, keep exploring. From there, you might discover and eventually create your own business,” Jonathan advised.
Ruhmaya Wathoni, B.BA, M.Sc., Director of Setsail BizAccel Presuniv and moderator of the event, agreed with Jonathan’s view. She added, “Starting your own business is not easy, but at Presuniv, we have various facilities to help students build their businesses. Setsail BizAccel is ready to assist students in starting their ventures.” (Graciella V, PR Team. Photo: Graciella V.)

Epicor Indonesia Initiates ERP Application Training for President University Students
September 24, 2025
Currently, technology plays a very strategic role in the business world. Almost all companies have implemented technology, both hardware and software, in their business processes. To anticipate this development, President University (Presuniv) collaborates with technology companies to prepare its students so that after graduation they have skills that match industry needs. One such collaboration is with PT Epicor Software Indonesia. Through this collaboration, Epicor provided assistance worth US$170,000 in the form of Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) software licenses and training programs for Presuniv students.
On Wednesday, July 17, 2024, the Territory Manager of Epicor Indonesia, Andi Mulyawan, initiated the ERP training program for students from the Information System (IS) Study Program, Faculty of Computer Science, Presuniv. Dozens of students participated in the training held at the Charles Himawan Auditorium, Building A, 5th Floor, Presuniv Campus, Jl. Ki Hajar Dewantara, Jababeka Education Park, Jababeka City, Cikarang, Bekasi.
According to Genta Sahuri M.Sc., an IS Study Program lecturer and ERP Project course instructor, “The ERP course and training are indeed aimed at making Presuniv stay one step ahead of other universities. Moreover, Presuniv is located in the Jababeka industrial area with thousands of companies within it. So, we can directly see real-world cases and the application of technology at these companies.”
ERP Widely Used
ERP is the most widely used application by companies worldwide. Through this application, companies can manage and integrate all their business processes. One key factor in implementing ERP is that all data and business processes within the company must be integrated into one platform and transformed into information. This information must be accessible by various departments or business units within the company. With complete and real-time information, companies can quickly make important business decisions.
The training in the use of ERP applications is provided to Presuniv students. According to Andi Mulyawan, ERP applications are widely used by companies in the manufacturing industry. “And, in the Jababeka industrial area, there are around 2,000 companies, most of which are in the manufacturing sector. So, if Presuniv graduates are proficient in using ERP applications, they will be quickly absorbed by these companies,” explained Andi.
To all participants, Andi advised them to truly utilize this training to develop their skills and knowledge in using the ERP applications. “Through this training, Presuniv has opened the door as wide as possible for all students to have skills and knowledge that meet industry needs. So, take advantage of this opportunity by learning and practicing earnestly,” said Andi. (Faisal Rabbani, PR team. Photo: Faisal Rabbani)

President Univ Collaborates with Jababeka and HRI Group to Host 2024 Mandarin Career Expo
September 13, 2025
On Friday, September 13, 2024, President University (Presuniv), in collaboration with PT Jababeka Tbk, Chindo Business Center, and HRI Group, held the 2024 Mandarin Career Expo at the President University Convention Center (PUCC), Jl. H. Usmar Ismail, Kota Jababeka, Cikarang. The event aimed to facilitate student’s and graduate’s careers and to raise awareness about the importance of learning multiple languages for their future career growth.
In his opening speech, Handa S. Abidin, S.H., LL.M., Ph.D., Rector of Presuniv, discussed the increasingly competitive job market. “Today, many students and graduates are fluent in both Indonesian and English. To enhance competitiveness, we need to learn other languages, such as Mandarin,” Handa explained. He added, “The Internship and Career Center at Presuniv has received many requests for students proficient in Mandarin, which reflects the demand for Mandarin-speaking workers in Indonesia today.”
The 2024 Mandarin Career Expo also featured performances by Presuniv students. Huang Yiting, a student from the 2023 Accounting study program and a native of China, showcased her talent in traditional Chinese dance in front of the attendees. Additionally, Jocelyn Shandy from the 2023 Communication study program displayed her Mandarin skills by performing the song Yue Liang Dai Biao Wo De Xin by Teresa Teng. (Graciella V, PR Team. Photo: Graciella V)

Study Program of Accounting, President Univ, Holds Seminar on the Role of Accountants in an AI-Driven Business with CPA Australia and Glico Indonesia
August 02, 2025
On Friday, August 2, 2024, the Study Program of Accounting, Faculty of Business, President University (Presuniv), in collaboration with CPA Australia and PT Glico Indonesia, organized the President University Accounting Day (PUAD). The event was held at the Charles Himawan Auditorium, 5th Floor, Building A, President University Campus on Jl. Ki Hajar Dewantara, Kota Jababeka, Cikarang, focusing on the theme “Role of Accountants in an AI-Driven Business.”
According to the Head of the Study Program of Accounting, Mila Austria Reyes, CPA, MBA, it is crucial for students to keep a close watch on the development of Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology. “What you learn during this seminar is vital for career development, including sharpening the vision of the career you will pursue in the future, especially after graduation,” said Mila.
The event featured two speakers: Oscar Syarif, Chief Operating Officer of Ledgerowl, and Melisa Yosefine, Financial Controller and Commercial Business Partner of PT Glico Indonesia. Melisa emphasized that students should not fear AI taking over their jobs. “Companies, whether big or small, will always need accountants because accounting is the language of business,” she said. Meanwhile, Oscar explained that AI is merely a tool to assist in work. “We cannot continue to work using traditional methods. We must evolve and adapt to technological advancements. This is crucial for our careers,” he stressed. (Graciella V, PR Team. Report: Evander Stevanus. Photos: Damar Satriawan).

President University Hosts Assed Lussak to Discuss Career Opportunities in International Communication
July 17, 2025
Recently, President University (Presuniv) welcomed Assed Lussak, founder and CEO of Strategic Nexus, to discuss career opportunities in international communication. The event was held in Room A429, 4th Floor, Building A, Presuniv Campus, Jl. Ki Hajar Dewantara, Kota Jababeka, Cikarang. It was moderated by Muhammad Sigit Andhi Rahman, Ph.D., a lecturer from the International Relations Study Program, Faculty of Social Sciences and Education, Presuniv.
M. Raudy Gathmyr, S.Sos., M.Si., Head of the Communication Study Program at Presuniv, emphasized that listening and understanding others are key aspects of communication. He stated, “Communication is a fundamental daily need and essential for career growth. This makes learning about communication crucial for all students, not just those in the Communication Study Program.”
Meanwhile, Assed highlighted that one promising international career path is becoming an international consultant. Given the diverse fields covered by international consulting firms, the demand for skilled professionals is high. However, Assed noted that students must pay attention to key factors if they aim to become international communication consultants. “Students need to stay updated on current trends, be proactive, and have confidence. Set career targets for yourself—this is the key to success,” he advised. (Graciella, PR Team. Photo: M. Sigit Andhi Rahman).

Internship & Career Center
What's Next After Graduation?
Our internship program serves as a facility for students to gain practical experience. By joining taking an internship program, students will learn and expose themselves to the variety of roles, functions, and abundance of opportunities in industries. This exposure generates a list of “career prospects” for the students, which will lead them to the answer of “what’s next after graduation”.
The quality of our graduates are measured by the abilities to create impact for the community and are reflected from their portfolios. This portfolio is made possible because as interns, our students will be part of the innovation and problem solving process where they can showcase their discipline, personal traits, knowledge and skills to the hiring manager and grow their professional network. This portfolio will be proof of their proficiency and adds value to them as professionals.
Why Internship?
In the end, our internship goal is “employability” which may be translated as the ability of our graduates to land their dream career as how they want it, when they need it. However, employability goes beyond getting hired, it is about constantly innovating and match the competencies with what industry or hiring organizations look for in a candidate. Combined with the academic mastery, the internship portfolio often helps students secure their job before graduation and stay relevant in a dynamic job market.
Internships are not just about fulfilling requirements, it is also about opening doors to the world of possibilities. Our internship journey shapes many success stories of graduates being offered with job opportunities right after they completed their internship.
Ready, Set, Get Hired
Resume Mastery
President University’s web-based Resume Builder guides you step by step through every section of your resume. Capture your key achievements, projects, work experience and certifications in one place. Each year the Career Center reviews your document to keep it aligned with current industry needs. The review involves personal coaching session to polish the final details, condense information, and shape a clear professional layout. The result is a concise one-page resume that you can use to apply for internships and jobs from your first semester, and one that stays competitive right up to graduation.
Internship & Job Opportunity Portal
One account gives you access to a curated list of internships and job openings from hundreds of partner companies. The portal (
https://linktr.ee/icc_pu
) is designed to boost employability by delivering a steady flow of high-quality opportunities for both students and alumni.
Career Fair
Our annual job fair brings many companies to campus and lets you apply and interview on the same day. We also host regular Campus Hiring events, each focused on a single employer for a more targeted recruitment experience. These programs combined, create a direct path from a strong resume to a signed employment contract.

PUSB

President University Student Board (PUSB)
President University Student Board (PUSB) established on August 23, 2008 in Jababeka, Cikarang. Based on the principle of from, by, and for the students, PUSU aims to enrich student life with constructive extracurricular activities and leadership development.
PUSB functions include:
Executing students’ aspirations as set by President University Student Council (PUSC).
Supporting and developing students’ potentials, talents, and interests.
Fostering a conducive academic and social environment.

Club and Communities
In President University, students get the facilities to develop their talent by joining Club and Communities
Society Club and Communities
President University fosters a vibrant campus life through a wide range of society clubs and communities that encourage intellectual exchange, cultural understanding, and social awareness. These student-led groups focus on issues such as leadership, public speaking, environmental sustainability, social justice, and international affairs. By participating in these communities, students are empowered to broaden their perspectives, engage in meaningful discussions, and take part in impactful projects that benefit both the campus and the wider society.
Discover →
Art Club and Communities
Creativity thrives at President University through its diverse art clubs and communities, which offer students a platform to explore and express their artistic talents. These groups include activities such as music, dance, theater, photography, visual arts, and creative writing. Whether performing on stage or displaying works in campus exhibitions, students are encouraged to celebrate artistic expression, collaborate across disciplines, and contribute to the university’s dynamic and inclusive cultural scene.
Discover →
Sport Club and Communities
Promoting a healthy and active lifestyle, President University provides numerous sport clubs and communities where students can train, compete, and build teamwork. From futsal and basketball to martial arts and badminton, these clubs support both recreational and competitive levels, often representing the university in regional and national tournaments. Through sports, students develop physical fitness, discipline, resilience, and strong camaraderie.
Discover →
Entrepreneurship Club
The Entrepreneurship Club at President University serves as a hub for aspiring innovators and business-minded students who are passionate about building their own ventures. Through workshops, pitch competitions, mentoring sessions, and collaboration with industry partners, the club nurtures entrepreneurial thinking and real-world problem-solving. It plays a key role in supporting students to transform ideas into viable startups and cultivating a future generation of leaders and changemakers.
Discover →

title: About – Adam Kurniawan Library
content:
About – Adam Kurniawan Library
A
DAM KURNIAWAN LIBRARY
Connect, Covered, Get Inspired | NPP: 3216092D0000001
Highlights
About
Profile
Rules & Regulations
Partnerships
Librarians
Collection
Books
Repository
E-resource
OER
Facilities
News
Adam Kurniawan Library
Connect, Covered, Get Inspired
Initiated by Mr. S.D. Darmono, one of the founders of President University, the university library
was first opened in 2003. As the university grew, a bigger space was needed to house the large
collection of books and materials as well serve the information needs of the library users and as a
result, it doubled in size.
The library was officially named The Adam Kurniawan Library in honor of Adam Kurniawan, who was
warmly known as “Pak Adam” by his friends and business associates. He was a respected entrepreneur
in the Indonesian textile industry and a co-founder of the Jababeka Group. He knew the importance of
helping others to achieve success in their lives. He was an important figure who had a great concern
for the nation’s education, the university and its library.
To help manage the library system as well as all the collection of books and materials, an IT-based
library information system called Suteki was installed and implemented in 2007 to serve the campus
community as well as all the library users.
Under the coordination and fund-raising activities of Dr. Agus Canny, the Vice Rector III of
President University, donations of library materials have been given from many sources such as
private institutions, Jababeka business partners, PATA, Indonesian ministries, Asia Foundation,
LPPM, Bank Indonesia and other organizations. Leaders of President University from the rectors,
heads of departments, faculty staff even the alumni of the university have also been involved in
donating library materials.
President University has grown a lot in the past few years and it recently became important to
expand the space of the library, the books and materials as well as its services to accommodate the
growing student body and achieve the vision of the university. The library started renovations in
May 2016 and it is now ready to be used as of August 2016 with its new phase of “home” and
“services” to serve the academic and literature needs of the university.
The design concept of the new library celebrates the UNESCO 4 pillars of Education: ‘Learning to
Know’ ‘Learning to Do’ ‘Learning to Be’ and ‘Learning to Live together’. These four pillars of
education are represented in the library by the four pillars of bookshelves and 4 big crystal lamps
hanging on the four corners above the book collection, as the heart of the library. The pillars in
the library are made of concrete and each pillar is covered with two mirrors with bare concrete in
the middle, and is equipped with wall lamps.
The bare concrete symbolizes that knowledge and wisdom starts from “raw” data while the wall lamps
represent education. These pillars with bare concrete, mirrors and lamps indicate that “raw” data
can be found in the library materials and it will be transformed into information and knowledge
through education which can be shared and multiplied by others. It is hoped that the library will be
used to maximize the learning process of our students as a fundamental part of their academic and
research endeavors at President University.
Our Vision
To be an information gateway and a facilitator of life-long learning in achieving the vision
of the university
Our Mission
The library is developing an IT-based system in managing and providing information
resources, both digital and hardcopy, as well as excellent services relevant to the needs of
the campus community.
The library is developing networks with campus community, both local and global centers of
information resources relevant to the needs of the campus community.
The library is working with its networks in providing information resources and integrated
information literacy programs.
The library is working with its networks in developing entertaining, cultural but
educational activities to develop life-long learning.

Rules and Regulations
Follow our library rules and regulation to get best learning experiences at
Adam Kurniawan Library
See also:
• General Rules
• Library Users
• Borrowing Book Procedures
• Library Clearance
General Rules
Respects to other users are highly appreciated, such as:
Keeping to a minimum volume of voices while talking or taking part in a group discussion
Sleeping, smoking, using cell phones, radio, joking, playing online games outside the
library.
Proper use of the library furniture.
Proper attired at all times in the library.
Keeping the library and the collection clean and in good condition by:
Eating and drinking outside the library.
Putting trash on the trash cans.
Proper handling of walls, furniture and library materials (no marking, ripping off,
breaking) etc.
Keeping the library in good order by:
Using the library mainly for academic and research purposes.
Putting the books on the trolley provided after reading or removing from the Shelves.
Leaving the furniture in its original place.
Not leaving belongings overnight in the library. The library shall not take any responsibility for
loss of personal property or books already issued out.
Report to the library immediately if you find other’s belongings or if you lose your belongings.
The library staff has the privilege to ask students to leave the library if they are behaving
inappropriately or causing disturbance to other users.
The library has the privilege to give any kind of discipline acts to any user who disregards the
library rules and regulations.
Who is Library user?
The following people reserve the right to be the members of the library.
Members are entitled to use all library services.
All registered students of the University,
Active Faculty and administrative staffs in the University,
Known members of the community and those with a genuine need,
have limited access to the library with a special permission from the head of the library.
** Others may read the library materials and use the reading room in the library.
Borrowing Book
Procedures
A user must be a registered student or active faculty and administrative staff in the current
term
to be able to use the library services.
All users must present their ID Card before borrowing any library materials.
Library materials may not be removed from the library unless the library staff has properly
issued them out.
All borrowed materials must be returned on or before the due date.
Students or staff are not allowed to borrow library materials on behalf of others or transfer
borrowed materials to others.
Students with overdue materials and overdue fines will not be allowed to borrow books.
Library Clearance
All students must upload their final project to the President University
Information System (PUIS). The final project will then be validated by the
Library Bureau staff under the following conditions:
i. Cover File
– named Cover_(StudentID).pdf (example: Cover_014201700175) and must include:
Title Page
Approval Page (with complete signatures)
Statement of Originality (signed and stamped)
Publication Release Form (signed)
Advisor's Approval for Publication (choose one format and signed by the advisor)
Turnitin Report and AI Turnitin Report showing total similarity percentage and AI-based plagiarism detection
Abstract
Acknowledgment Page
Table of Contents
List of Tables (if any)
List of Figures/Diagrams (if any)
The required templates for the Title Page, Approval Page, Statement of Originality, Publication Release Form, and Advisor's Approval for Publication are available at
bit.ly/LibraryClearance
ii. Chapter File
– named Chapter_(StudentID).pdf (example: Chapter_014201700175), maximum file size 3 MB.
iii. References File
– named References_(StudentID).pdf (example: References_014201700175).
iv. Panel Examiner Approval Sheet
– named Approval_(StudentID).pdf (example: Approval_014201700175).
Students must return all borrowed books and pay any overdue fines (if applicable).
Students are required to submit the
hard copy of the Approval Page with original signatures
to the library in order to receive validation in the PUIS system.
Notes:
A. Any student who intends to participate in the graduation ceremony must first complete all Library Clearance requirements as stated in the regulations.
B. Failure to comply with the Library Clearance requirements, as stipulated in this
Rector's Regulation, will result in the loss of the right to participate in the graduation
ceremony.
Library Clearance
In order to obtain the Library Clearance for thesis submission, students who have completed thesis must
follow the required procedures and steps as below:
Submission of thesis soft file
to PUIS in
.pdf
format, and must comply
with the following thesis structure, layout, and requirement:
File
Cover_student ID number.pdf
(e.g.
Cover_014201700175.pdf
)
a. Cover & Title Sheet
b. Approval Sheet (fully signed)
c. Originality Sheet (signed)
d. Publication Handover Sheet (signed)
e. Advisor Approval Sheet for publication in the journal/repository/ proceeding/
unpublished (Choose one and advisor signed)
f. The results of the Turnitin similarity check (Page total similarities only)
g. The results of the anti-plagiarism base on Artificial Intelligence through
software like GPTZero (
https://gptzero.me
)
h. Abstract
i. Acknowledge Page
j. Table of Contents
k. List of Tables (optional)
l. List of Pictures/Diagrams (optional)
** Link for point a, b, c, d, and e sheet:
https://bit.ly/LibraryClearance
File
Chapter1–Last Chapter_student ID number.pdf
, maximal 3Mb (e.g.
Chapter1-5_014201700175.pdf
)
File
References_student ID number.pdf
(e.g.
References_014201700175.pdf
)
Panel Examiner Approval Sheet. Name it
Approval Student ID Number
(e.g.
Approval_014201700175.pdf
).
THE SIGNATURE SHOULD BE ORIGINAL, GIVE THE PAPER TO THE ADMIN STUDY PROGRAM TO GET
VALIDATION.
No reference liability.

OPEN EDUCATIONAL RESOURCES (OER)
Learning and teaching materials that can be shared, edited, and reused without obtaining permission from the
owner are known as Open Educational Resources, or OERs.
Since the producer of OER material has lifted certain copyright restrictions, it is readily accessible to
the public and uses under an open license in the public domain.
For academics looking for a free and authorized alternative information source to aid in their research and
study, President University OER is available.
The following are the tenets of OER:
OER documents can be downloaded, printed, and saved using retain.
Reuse refers to the acceptance of content reuse.
Using revise, you can modify the material to fit your requirements.
Blending several OER materials together to create a new work is made feasible by remix.
Redistribute refers to the sharing of content in either its original or altered format.
